programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A visitor dies in Dr Sloan's house, but the body disappears before the police arrive, raising fears that the medic imagined it all. Drama, starring Dick Van Dyke and Scott Baio

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Illusionist Eddie Clark gives Mark free tickets to his final show in the Magic Mansion before he and his partner Paul Madison go their separate ways. However, Paul plans to lay claim to all of their tricks when he goes solo, before an escapology stunt goes wrong and he dies on stage. Mark is unwilling to believe that Eddie could be guilty of murder, and decides to investigate. Guest starring Emma Samms

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: While working as a gas station attendant, Kimble and a young woman are taken hostage when two trigger-happy hoods hold up the station

programme.title: Perry Mason

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Rangers go undercover in an attempt to stop a group of gangsters unlawfully taking ownership of a nightclub. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A drug lord is gunned down in his mansion, and Delko faces a major threat to his career when one of the suspects claims to have sold marijuana to him. Stetler from Internal Affairs investigates and the case takes on a personal dimension once Horatio gets involved. Meanwhile, Calleigh makes a major decision concerning her job

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The driver of a Ferrari is gunned down by a motorcyclist and the victim is identified as petty officer Colin Boxer, who was holding down a second job as a valet parker and had borrowed the sports car. The owner of the vehicle turns out to be Vijay Chaya, the billionaire head of a large corporation, and the team suspects a case of mistaken identity. During the course of the investigation, Gibbs chances on a medal awarded to Leroy Jethro Moore, the man he was named after and an estranged friend of his father. Guest starring Billy Dee Williams (The Empire Strikes Back) and Ralph Waite (The Waltons)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Marine is found murdered in Quantico, mere hours after calling Gibbs to discuss a possible case. In response, Bishop and McGee go undercover as a married couple to run surveillance on a lieutenant and his wife, who may be connected to the death. Starring Mark Harmon, Emily Wickersham and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Aiden try to solve the mystery of a stockbroker's death by analysing a cryptic message on his computer. Meanwhile, Stella investigates the case of a pregnant counsellor whose body has been found near the church where she worked. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise, Vanessa Ferlito and Melina Kanakaredes

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Stella investigate the fatal shooting of a mounted police officer - but the only evidence is a bullet lodged in his horse, presenting them with a dilemma. Aiden takes on the case of a dead youth discovered with severe facial burns, and soon finds herself trying to crack an illegal betting operation

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team helps Cabe when he receives a distress code from his ex-wife Rebecca. She fears she is being targeted for stumbling upon a money trail, which has links to the assassination of a congressman. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: A jet carrying a classified cloaking radar device goes down over Bosnia, and the team helps Navy Seals to find the aircraft so they can recover the technology and locate the pilot's remains. Meanwhile, Walter is forced to bring his sister to meet the team after she has a drunken run-in with the police. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents are called to the scene of a car accident in which a petty officer was killed. They discover a bullet wound in the body, as well as another victim at the bottom of a nearby ravine - a woman who was beaten to death. Ducky conducts an autopsy and makes a discovery that sets the agents off in search of a missing infant. Meanwhile, Tony is distracted by developments in his relationship with Jeanne

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Lt Col Mann arrives to assist with inquiries into the murder of a marine - a case that becomes personal for Gibbs when he learns one of the witnesses is his ex-wife Stephanie. The team discovers the prime suspect was having an affair with the dead man's spouse, but the case is complicated by the revelation that the victim may have been selling top-secret military information. Starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: While performing an autopsy for a class of military students, Ducky discovers the cadaver he is using is the body of a murder victim. In trying to identify the corpse, Gibbs and his team are set on the trail of a career criminal specialising in identity fraud. Meanwhile, Ziva has difficulty separating her personal feelings and professional judgment. Starring David McCallum and Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Navy Seal is shot dead while threatening to throw himself from the top of a building. An al-Qaeda operative emerges as the main suspect, but DiNozzo thinks the killer may actually be found closer to home. Elsewhere, a rumour spreads that Abby may be about to leave NCIS for good. Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette star in the American drama

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The unit is called to examine the mysterious death of a sailor serving on a research vessel engaged in a top-secret mission. The ship proves to have been abandoned by its crew, and Ducky discovers the victim died from a highly contagious virus - putting everyone presently on board at risk. Drama, starring David McCallum, Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Lauren Holly

However, the photograph is leaked, and Roe is later found dead with a needle in his arm

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Director Vance charges DiNozzo with a special assignment to go to France, locate Admiral Kendall's wayward daughter Amanda and bring her home. Arriving in Marseille, Tony immediately heads to the local NCIS office, only to find five dead agents. After eventually tracking down Amanda, the pair embark on a rollercoaster ride to the relative safety of the nearest NCIS office in Spain. Michael Weatherly stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs travels to his home town after hearing news of his father's death, while the team investigates whether a fire on a US Navy ship was accidental or planned. The vessel served as prison to several members of the Brotherhood of Doubt terrorist group, whom Gibbs was responsible for catching. After the blaze, two of those members are missing. Tony argues that Gibbs would want to know, but Vance does not want him disturbed at this difficult time. Mark Harmon stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Known stalker Larry Franks emerges as prime suspect in the Cindy Statham murder case - until CCTV footage reveals the model had a furious row in a restaurant with her half-brother on the day she died. Leela reports an attack by an intruder but she is put in a compromising situation when her colleagues turn up and discover a bag of heroin in her flat

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Donna's father's will sends her on a voyage of discovery as she and Maria follow a set of mysterious instructions that leads them to a woman and child. Linden tries to work out why Connie, Ric and Faye were suspended as the police begin their investigation into Archie's death, and as Judith implements new infection control procedures she comes to realise what an asset she has in Mark

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Abs has second thoughts about refusing to marry Ellen as he urges her to corroborate his version of events when questioned about the cardiac arrest case. Comfort discharges herself from hospital following her pregnancy scare, determined to keep her condition secret, but on returning to work her world is rocked by a shocking revelation

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Terry goes undercover, hoping to arrest a supplier of illegally imported goods, but gets caught up in a pub brawl between rival football fans in which a fellow officer is stabbed. Lewis's inside knowledge is called upon when another spate of gang violence breaks out and the new leader of the Skens appears to be settling old scores. Guest starring Adrian Lukis

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Peggy's return to the Vic catches Frank and Ricky on the hop. Meanwhile, Mark's attempts to celebrate Pauline's birthday are overshadowed by Willie

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Frank realises that the time has come to make a decision about his future and Ricky's attempts to sort out his life lead him to turn to his father for advice

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: Paddy and Dave plan a betting scam when the top boxer Corporal Tony Wilton is knocked out of the team, but their scheme falls apart when Wilton is recalled

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: Henry cooks a banquet for a visiting Japanese delegation, and tracks down a criminal from his past, hitherto presumed dead. This shocking discovery prompts him to investigate corruption in the police force. Richard Griffiths stars

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Siegfried is determined to make errant farmers pay their bills, but his idea does not go exactly to plan. Rural drama, starring Robert Hardy and Christopher Timothy

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: DeVere moves his multinational grocery business to the manor and clashes with Audrey over the removal of a fireplace that he considers ugly and she believes is intrinsic to the property. Comedy, starring Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: A transport strike forces the staff of Grace Brothers to spend the night in the store. Shop-floor comedy, starring John Inman, Mollie Sugden and Trevor Bannister

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: The women of the village are impressed by a series of fanciful tales told by a retired jewel thief about his larcenous exploits in the south of France. However, the scoundrel's skills are put to the test when Marina and Miss Davenport seek his aid in helping Auntie Wainwright crack a keyless safe. John Challis guest stars

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: When Alvin and Entwistle witness Lance attempting to prove to his wife that Elvis Presley is still alive, the duo feel duty-bound to help him out, and organise for the King himself to make an appearance. Long-running comedy starring Brian Murphy, Burt Kwouk and Kenneth Cope

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A missing woman staggers into the police station, claiming a creature is 'trying to get out'. That night her father panics as he sees an alien shape through the window. The next day his body is found in a locked room and Father Brown suspects he has been poisoned. Such events prompt local speculation to mount that Kembleford is under threat of an alien invasion. Mark Williams stars

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team investigates an arson attack on a 1980s music station that resulted in the death of a controversial DJ, and looks into the subsequent suicide of the station's owner. Strickland seeks a permanent replacement for the absent Halford, but Pullman and Standing call his bluff

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Another mystery for Ardal O'Hanlon as Irish detective Jack Mooney working on the Caribbean island of Saint Marie, where the sun shines all day and it seems half the residents are killers-in-waiting. And so it appears this week as someone from JP's past is caught red-handed at a murder scene, raking up old memories for the young officer. But can the DI unravel the complex mystery to get at the truth? Tobi Bakare and Danny John-Jules co-star, with guest appearances from Ralph Brown and Adjoa Andoh

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The body of police hero Debbie Britten washes up in the Thames a year after she disappeared, summoning the Cold Case unit to investigate. Initially, suspicion falls on a stalker, but the plot thickens further with the discovery that Debbie was pregnant at the time of her murder. Trevor Eve, Sue Johnston, Holly Aird and Tessa Peake-Jones star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Terry goes undercover, hoping to arrest a supplier of illegally imported goods, but gets caught up in a pub brawl between rival football fans in which a fellow officer is stabbed. Lewis's inside knowledge is called upon when another spate of gang violence breaks out and the new leader of the Skens appears to be settling old scores. Guest starring Adrian Lukis

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: The Act of Supremacy has declared Henry the supreme head of the church in England, but Holy Roman emperor Charles V and his ambassador Eustache Chapuys refuse to recognise the new title or the monarch's divorce and subsequent marriage. With Anne pregnant and away from court, Henry begins to take more notice of Jane Seymour, whose family enlist Cromwell's help in their dealings with the king - much to Anne's displeasure. Historical drama, starring Mark Rylance, Damian Lewis, Clare Foy and Mathieu Amalric

Medical drama, starring Christopher Timothy and Martina Laird

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: After making a complaint about Gina's bullying behaviour, Emma is embarrassed to find herself working alongside the inspector on a tricky rape allegation in which the evidence does not seem to add up. programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ricky rescues Phil from the fire at the portacabin. Sam and Bianca rush to visit them in hospital. Frank is suspected but nowhere to be seen

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: The police investigate the car lot fire as Phil receives visitors in hospital. Meanwhile, it's Pauline's birthday and Pat makes a decision after a disturbing phone call

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: The company are put through a specialist training exercise, supervised by Veteran explosives expert, Sergeant Lewis. Starring Robson Green and Jerome Flynn

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: When a partner in a snail-importing business is murdered, Crabbe suspects the firm has a sideline. Richard Griffiths stars

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Calum becomes more enthusiastic when he meets Emma Styles, the new veterinary student. Rural drama, starring Robert Hardy, Christopher Timothy and John McGlynn

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: DeVere neglects to invite Audrey to the annual Hunt Ball - so she plans a socially acceptable revenge. Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles star

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: A glamorous perfume saleswoman arrives on the shop floor, prompting a disgruntled Mrs Slocombe to plot the newcomer's downfall. Comedy, guest starring Joanna Lumley

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: When Eva, a worldly wise former acquaintance of Alvin arrives in the village, while Marina discovers she might have some competition as the apple of Howard's eye. Long running comedy, guest starring Shirley Ann Field

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Barry tries to play matchmaker when he discovers that his neighbour Chislehurst is desperately lonely. However, the task soon proves difficult, and Barry quickly discovers that elegible women are hard to come by. Long-running comedy, starring Mike Grady and Tyler Butterworth

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Flambeau is incarcerated under suspicion of murder, but he denies killing his cohort Flynn and asks Father Brown to hear his confession if he is convicted

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A popular reggae band reunite after 30 years to perform on Saint Marie, but the joyous occasion is blighted when guitarist Billy Springer is found dead in his dressing room afterwards. With a gun in his hand and the door locked from the inside, all evidence points toward suicide - but when the Commissioner reveals he received a message from the victim earlier in the day, the case becomes rather more complicated. Meanwhile, Dwayne struggles to come to terms with his dad's surprise visit. Is it too late for them to make amends? With guest appearances by Ram John Holder, Suzanne Packer, Delroy Brown and Levi Roots

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The apparent suicide of a 1970s guitarist is investigated following the deathbed confession of a witness. Strickland, a fan of the rock star's band, reveals himself to be a keen musician, while Brian tracks down Jack and persuades him to rejoin Ucos. With guest appearances by Roger Lloyd Pack and George Costigan

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. When the only living victim of a jailed serial killer starts being harassed by a stalker, Boyd is given the chance to prove his theory that the murderer had a partner who is still on the loose. Starring Trevor Eve and Sue Johnston

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Anne accuses Cromwell of betrayal when she finds out he tried to protect Mary and not Elizabeth at a time of crisis, but the queen's power is dissolving rapidly and her enemies are gathering. Her sister-in-law Jane Rochford reveals to Cromwell that Mark Smeaton, Francis Weston and Henry Norris have all declared their love to Anne, which is a treasonable offence, and further insinuates that her husband George Boleyn's unhealthy sexual appetite extends to his sister. Historical drama, starring Mark Rylance, Claire Foy and Jessica Raine

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: The sleuthing 12th-century monk investigates a murder as the Abbey becomes involved in the war between King Stephen and the Empress Maud. The trail of clues leads him to a cache of hidden treasure and the fleeing daughter of the castle's owner, Godith. Medieval mystery, starring Derek Jacobi

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: David plans a surprise party for Karen's 40th birthday, even though she'd rather not bother, and Anna rekindles her romance with Simon now he's returned from Australia a single man once more. Jess is devastated to receive disappointing A level results, meaning she can't get away from home, and newcomers the Morecroft family arrive in Skelthwaite, hoping to shake off their tragic past

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Stephen is faced with a dilemma when he meets an old flame. Karen and David begin to worry about the whereabouts of Jess, and an elderly patient is distraught when his partner is put into care. Drama following community nurses living and working in a small Yorkshire town, starring Leslie Ash, Phil Middlemiss and Thomas Craig

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Back on English soil after his recent escapades in France, Sharpe is assigned to restore peace in the north, where the local aristocracy's corrupt and high-handed ways have led to mutterings of rebellion among the poor. And as the unrest grows, he's forced to decide where his true loyalties lie. Feature-length adventure, starring Sean Bean, Daragh O'Malley, Abigail Cruttenden, and Caroline Langrishen

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: A spate of petty pilfering in the hut leads to fears that one of the lads may be a thief - and prompts Oz to come up with a novel way of catching the culprit. Classic comedy, starring Kevin Whately, Jimmy Nail and Timothy Spall

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: Bomber learns his daughter has run away, and immediately flies home to lend his wife moral support - unaware the young lady in question is winging her way to Dusseldorf to be with him. Comedy drama, starring Pat Roach, Tim Healy, Gary Holton and Jimmy Nail

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: Brenda makes it clear that she thinks Neville is living it up in Germany just as a leather-clad local girl, whom he merely shared a taxi with, accuses him of assaulting her. Cult comedy drama, starring Kevin Whately, Tim Healy and Julia Tobin

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Laura and Rosemary are asked to restore an ornate water garden cascade in the grounds of a health spa, where they are invited to join owner Frances Caldecott and her children for dinner. That evening, the duo discover Frances has been strangled and her jewellery stolen, and set out to catch the culprit. Drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The duo are hired to cure a patchy lawn at a select language school hiding dark secrets. The disappearance of a Polish student alerts them to a tale of passion, rivalry and revenge, and when a handyman is found with his throat cut they race to find the killer. Drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: During a performance of Hamlet, young cast member Ben Faulkner collapses and dies. Initially the detectives think Ben died of natural causes, until Mike uncovers a complex web of ego-driven dissension among the troupe and follows his nose to the real cause of death

programme.title: Miss Fisher's Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: Phryne gets more than she bargained for when her Aunt Prudence opens her home to psychiatrist and natural healer Hayden Samuels to conduct his sanatorium for 'hysterical' women

programme.title: WPC 56
programme.desc: Drama centred on the fortunes of police officer Gina Dawson, who faces an uphill battle to carve out a successful career for herself in the male-dominated world of the 1950s. Brinford Police Station is rocked by a shocking discovery in an abandoned mine, while two senior officers investigate an attack on a young woman. Jennie Jacques and John Light star

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Jardine and Taggart investigate the murder of a pensioner, focusing their inquiries on his daughter and son-in-law. Unfortunately, the case soon takes an unexpected turn. Glasgow-set detective drama, starring Mark McManus and Blythe Duff

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A body is discovered in the boot of a vehicle that has just been through the car crusher, presenting Taggart and Jardine with a case that seems to connect the worlds of drug dealers and artists. Mark McManus and James Macpherson star

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Drama set in 1895, chronicling the personal and professional consequences of Eleanor Bramwell's determined quest to become the first female surgeon at the East London Hospital. The first episode sees Eleanor embarking on her medical career _ and immediately crossing swords with eminent surgeon Sir Herbert Hamilton. Jemma Redgrave stars in the title role, with David Calder as her father, and Robert Hardy as Sir Herbert

programme.desc: Specialist topics are the history of Bro Trawsfynydd, the Gilmore Girls TV series, Terry Pratchett's The City Watch novels and the mountains of Wales. Presented by Betsan Powys

programme.title: Newyddion a Chwaraeon
programme.desc: Weekend news and sport

programme.title: Clwb Rygbi
programme.desc: Cardiff Blues v Scarlets. A chance to see in full the PRO14 match at the Arms Park

programme.title: Noson Lawen
programme.desc: A host of talented singers and comedians come together to celebrate Dafydd Iwan's 75th birthday on the Noson Lawen. With Gwyneth Glyn, Twm Morys, Ifan Gruffydd, Delwyn Siôn, The Welsh Whisperer, Seingar Choir and the Bro Teifi School Choir. Presented by Elin Fflur

programme.title: Priodas Pum Mil
programme.desc: Trystan Ellis-Morris and Emma Walford lend a helping hand to a crew of family and friends who are organising a wedding in Pwllheli for local couple Vicky and Chris

programme.title: Lwp: Curadur
programme.desc: Jazz and R'n'B singer Eadyth Crawford from Merthyr Tydfil curates this week's show, revealing the artists that have influences her, including Shamoniks, Ladies of Rage, Izzy Rabey and Endaf

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: The holiday-makers left footing the bill after insurers refused to pay for their medical emergencies, and the bizarre cases of travellers sent to stay in hotels that did not exist

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The redevelopment of a million-pound-plus house in Fulham, as well as the progress of auction buys in St Helens near Liverpool and Stoke in Staffordshire

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Rebecca Ellis had always dreamt of a move to Australia, inspired by her grandfather's tales of a beautiful and friendly country. Partner Kylie and her four children wanted to support her, but fears of leaving loved ones and the kids' beloved father behind were foremost in their minds. Could a week in Brisbane help them come round to Rebecca's way of thinking? Three years since we last caught up with them, where do they call home - Australia or the UK?

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a single glove uncovered the identities of a prolific and violent gang, and an image of a distinctive ponytail that solved a high-tech bank heist

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Kate Bliss help the teams in Stamford, Lincolnshire, and Natasha Raskin Sharp visits Burghley House to look at The Hell Staircase, a fine Baroque mural

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The sleuthing clergyman uncovers secrets, hypocrisy and murder at a finishing school for young ladies. Mystery, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers cook comfort food oozing with traditional values, including a twist on a classic pudding and a perfect teatime treat

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin is on a house-hunting mission with a couple who have £750,000 to break away from their London suburb and find a rural family home in North Yorkshire

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Proud farmers Jim and Stuart compete in one of the UK's oldest and most prestigious sheepdog trials at Longshaw - but the competition is strong in this part of the Peak District. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers are offered to chance to bid on a vintage stereoscope, two gigantic apothecary glasses and a children's game thought to be from the 19th century

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Patrick's good intentions catch up with him, leaving Denise furious and him heartbroken. Ruby makes a snap admission in an attempt to keep Martin close, while Martin is inspired by Stacey's strength. Gray tells Karen that he wants his kids back for good

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: Dog fanatic Sheridan Smith and her fluffy co-host Stanley are on a mission to find the nation's best professional dog groomer. Heat one sees the first four contestants working on a long-haired shih-tzu - one of the most popular breeds to grace British salons. After that they let their imaginations fly as they give four curly haired canines a teddy bear trim to parade on the dogwalk in front of their owners

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Caribbean-set whodunit returns for its 10th anniversary series, which promises more murders and the return of one or two familiar faces. A TV news presenter is found dead in her pool at home. DI Neville Parker is convinced her co-host is responsible. But given he was live on air at the time, how could he have done it? The return of an experienced sergeant to the island could be just what Neville needs to crack the case

programme.title: The Limehouse Golem (2016)
programme.desc: After a series of violent murders scandalises Victorian London, the media-dubbed Limehouse Golem strikes fear into the hearts of the public. Into this melting pot is thrust well-intentioned inspector John Kildare, who has never been given a murder case before. The Scotland Yard sleuth knows he has been set up to fail, but soon uncovers a lead that implicates Karl Marx, George Gissing and the popular music hall star Dan Leno. He follows the trail, wherever it may lead, through the seediest and most intriguing parts of London. Thriller, starring Bill Nighy, Douglas Booth and Olivia Cooke

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: The music fans stuck with tickets for concerts abroad that were cancelled due to coronavirus, and the static caravan owners whose holiday havens are headed for the junkyard

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell and Martin Roberts follow the progress of terraced houses in Frizington in Cumbria and Wolverhampton and a huge commercial building in Stoke-on-Trent

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: After losing her father, Cheryl Castling was determined to live life to the fullest, and thought that a move to Oz could be the adventure she and her husband Adam needed. Adam was less than convinced, despite being the one to have the idea in the first place. Could a week of living the Aussie life make him see where Cheryl was coming from? Four years on, have they been able to come to a decision about where home should be? Presented by Nicki Chapman

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A plastic bag heat sealer that convicted an online drug trafficker. Plus, a casual chat with a criminal and a map join the dots to catch three burglars terrorising a neighbourhood

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion goes upstairs and downstairs looking back at the buys that best reveal Britain's class divide. The show compares items used by the wealthy and those working below stairs and also visits a workhouse to look at those who turned their back on domestic service

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Mallory and Father Brown go missing, and it is up to the gang to save them from a man hell-bent on revenge. Mark Williams and Jack Deam star in the crime drama

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers prepare dishes to feed a multitude. Their salmon pie with spinach and hollandaise is a showstopper, while their chocolate and cherry buns make a perfect centrepiece

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah is on a mission to help find a country home for £300,000 in the East Midlands for an eager buyer who has brought along her friendly neighbour for support. While in the region, Sonali pays a visit to an Edwardian house in Worksop, now managed by the National Trust, and steps back in time to learn about everyday life and work in the region during that era

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Husband-and-wife cider-makers Liz and Mike are excited to bring their cider, made from eight varieties of apples, to the South of England Agricultural Show. Meanwhile, Andy, his wife Anna and their two young children Lillie and Daisy have spent the last few days gearing up for the show's poultry competition. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Deep in the Yorkshire countryside, Nigel Havers welcomes five people with intriguing pieces they are hoping to sell. To help them before they face the dealers, Nigel introduces them to resident valuation expert Simon, who has been an auctioneer for nearly 30 years. Dealers then bid for a lamp, Victorian wall mirror, Black Forest inkwell and a quirky sideboard

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants have to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Harry Judd host the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich set about transforming a sea of dingy gravel and paving for a couple eager for a peaceful haven. Owners Andrew and Dawn also want a design that allows the family caravan to be accommodated. Whose design will fall on stony ground?

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Martin gives Sonia some words of encouragement as they share their problems. Ben finds himself in a tricky situation at the police station. Linda hopes she has made the right decision and Denise is in for another shock - but is everything as it seems?

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell are joined by former Premier League midfielder Steve Sidwell, Winter Olympic snowboarder Aimee Fuller, 2019 World Indoor champion sprinter Richard Kilty, and netball star Pamela Cookey. Sue Barker asks the questions

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Return of the comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Taking part in this first edition are Les Dennis, Richard Osman, Alice Levine and Lou Sanders

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Social niceties are stretched to snapping point when Lee and Lucy take in a neighbour's delivery for safekeeping. Lee unknowingly pulls apart the packaging, only to discover it's a somewhat personal item. To top it off, there's no way to hide the fact it's been opened. Thus, getting it back to its rightful owner proves easier said than done. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton, with Hugh Dennis

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Peep Show duo David Mitchell and Robert Webb, who have reunited for a new series of Back. Oscar-winner Regina King discusses making her directing debut with One Night in Miami, while actor Anya Taylor-Joy talks about hit chess drama The Queen's Gambit. Singer Olly Alexander reflects on starring in the new Russell T Davies series It's a Sin, and US comedian Jimmy Fallon, host of The Tonight Show. Plus, '; comedian Mel Giedroyc promotes new panel show Unforgivable and singer-songwriter Yungblud performs the single Cotton Candy

programme.title: War of the Worlds (2005)
programme.desc: A divorced father's rare weekend with his two estranged children descends into horror when alien war machines rise from beneath Earth and begin to systematically destroy every human being in their path - leaving the man with the dangerous task of getting his kids to safety. Steven Spielberg's sci-fi thriller, based on HG Wells' novel, with Tom Cruise, Dakota Fanning, Justin Chatwin, Miranda Otto and Tim Robbins

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: National and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45. Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Adam Byatt and Anna Haugh, and guest Tamzin Outhwaite, while drinks expert Olly Smith selects the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: The chef reveals the Cornwall that he knows and loves, championing the food, history, music, art and culture of the county,. He begins at his family home in Trevose Head on the north coast, where as a child he would go fishing with his father

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to what is one of the most eagerly anticipated weekends of the football calendar, when the FA Cup third round takes place

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross challenges two teams in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, to scour two antique centres and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction in Norfolk. Experts Jonathan Pratt and Colin Young offer support, while away from the action Charlie sees a selection of of rare toys

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley meets a couple who are looking to lay down roots in Devon after decades serving in the Army, with a £400,000 budget to find their ideal home near the English Riviera. Away from the property hunt, Ginny capitalises on being in Devon at the same time as the County Show to find out what it takes to win Pig of the Year

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades scours the recycling centre in Altrincham, Greater Manchester, in search of three skip-bound castoffs he can rework and repurpose. Woodworking specialist Bruce Faulseit is on hand to give an old bookcase a new look, while upholsterer Ray Clarke attempts a creative makeover on a set of dining chairs. For his own project, Jay has a plant stand to spruce up

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the FA Cup, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Arsenal v Newcastle United (Kick-off 5.30pm). Coverage of the third-round fixture, which takes place at Emirates Stadium, as the holders take on Steve Bruce's Magpies. The Gunners have struggled badly in the Premier League so far this season, and may see this competition as a welcome distraction. The sides last met in this tournament at the fourth-round stage in 2008, when an Emmanuel Adebayor double and a Nicky Butt own goal sealed a routine 3-0 victory for Arsene Wenger's team

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jacob wants more from Connie, but she is unsure if will she be able to make the time for their relationship with mounting pressures on the emergency department. Meanwhile, Faith confronts Lev about his secret, and new paramedic Leon arrives to meet the rest of the team and complete a Covid-19 training course

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: Award-winning comedian Michael McIntyre hosts, as contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing the cash prize

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: When a handsome man appears in Foley's Pub, everyone presumes he must have set his attentions on Cathy. However, the stranger reveals he only has eyes for one very special woman - none other than Agnes Brown. Will the doting mum-of-six agree to go on her first date in donkey's years? And if she does, who is in for the bigger surprise - Agnes or the stranger? Festive instalment of the comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll, with Dermot O'Neill, Danny O'Carroll and Jennifer Gibney

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Action from the third round, including Manchester United v Watford at OId Trafford, Aston Villa v Liverpool at Villa Park and Boreham Wood v Millwall at Meadow Park. Plus, all the goals and major talking points from the day's other fixtures, including Everton v Rotherham United, Chorley v Derby County, Exeter City v Sheffield Wednesday, Queens Park Rangers v Fulham, Southampton v Shrewsbury Town and Burnley v Milton Keynes Dons

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present a preview of the Wild Card play-off games, as the post-season begins

programme.title: End of Watch (2012)
programme.desc: Two Los Angeles cops make their regular patrol of the city, dealing with petty offences while one records their lives on camera for a documentary project. The officers end up in over their heads when they stumble on evidence that could bring down a drug cartel, putting them squarely in the gang's firing line. Crime thriller, with Jake Gyllenhaal and Michael Pena

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Lee Price was a huge fan of the Down Under lifestyle, having fallen in love with the place after getting married there. While he thought a move to New Zealand would be a great idea, he needed to convince wife Amanda and son Aaron, who didn't have the same enthusiasm. Could a trial week give them a glimpse into what they might be able to achieve? Three years on, where are the Prices based now?

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two sets of farmers - Jack and Nina and Andy and Tim - hope to make a splash with their rapeseed oils and sauces at a market in the small town of Axbridge, Somerset

programme.title: 12 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Shih tzu-pug cross Miska moves on to a houseboat with new owner Scarlett. After signs that Miska is adapting well to her new home, Scarlett takes her to work, but she is shocked when the dog ignores her and starts yapping at her colleagues in the open-plan office. In Surrey, the Summerscale-Heards continue training St Bernard pup Woody to offer assistance to five-year-old Dexter, and in West Yorkshire, Phoebe the cavapoo joins retirees Linda and Andrew - only to start nipping and jumping up

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Rob Beckett, in which contestants Yasmin, Michael and Dubem must choose between working together or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves

programme.title: Coast and Country Auctions
programme.desc: A visit to Exeter Livestock Market, the biggest agricultural auction in the region, where a team of auctioneers can sell more than 3,000 animals in about four hours

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Antiques experts Christina Trevanion and Mark Stacey continue their rivalry at Newark Antique Fair, where he buys some kitsch poodles while she chooses a silver piggy

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces recipes that can be prepared quickly, including Nigel Slater's herb butter squid, James Martin's icebox cake and Mary Berry's chocolate tray bake

programme.title: Made in Great Britain
programme.desc: Steph McGovern leads the makers through the ages from the 17th century to the present day, revealing how Stoke-on-Trent dominated the pottery industry, transforming from a collection of rural villages to a city that became the world capital of ceramics. The makers are first tasked with making elementary butter pots, before moving on to an oblong-shaped tea canister, decorated with blue cobalt pigment. They also study the invention of Bone China, use a 'jiggering' machine to shape clay into a plate and visit celebrated pottery company Wedgwood

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Trouble brews among the meerkats as the impact of two simultaneous pregnancies makes its mark. Youssarian is left to babysit Flower's second litter of the year, while Mozart tries to assert her authority on the Whiskers. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People in Weston-super-Mare bring their valuables along to the Grand Pier for valuation by experts Jonathan Pratt and Thomas Plant. Plus, presenter Paul Martin discovers more about the seaside pier itself

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Gemma Cairney, Tim Key, Gabby Logan and Jeff Stelling test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick discovers an unusual superstition and a rare type of Norman castle in Launceston, before meeting musicians Graham Fitkin and Ruth Wall in the far west of Cornwall

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: A brother and sister team fish for oysters in a traditional wooden sailing boat on the Fal estuary - but this is not enough to support them, so now they plan to trade it in for a small trawler. Further down river in St Mawes, a veteran trawler captain reflects on a past brush with death and the changing face of his home town, as working boats give way to yachts

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: In the Solent, one the busiest stretches of water in the UK, reports come in of a missing kite surfer. The crew of Lymington lifeboat station start searching, but as the hours pass they begin to fear the worst. Twenty miles to the west, the Poole crew are called out to save a father and daughter from drowning as they are swept out to sea, and at Chiswick, one of the few RNLI stations with a crew on permanent standby 24/7, an unusual casualty in the Thames leaves the crew attempting to save not just one but nine lives

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig asks questions on animals beginning with the letter R, with Tom Allen, Ed Gamble, Maggie Aderin-Pocock and Alan Davies trying to come up with interesting answers - while avoiding the most obvious ones

programme.title: A Teacher
programme.desc: Eric and Claire are slowly forced to confront the full extent of the damage that their relationship has inflicted on them and everyone around them

programme.title: A Teacher
programme.desc: Ten years on, Eric is working with emotionally vulnerable children, while Claire has children of her own. Returning to Austin for his high school reunion, Eric is brought back, after far too long, to his old friends and to Alison. It feels good, but another reunion, inevitably, is soon to follow

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: MasterChef judge Gregg Wallace and mathematician Hannah Fry take over a restaurant and invite five celebrities to enjoy a dinner party with a difference, where they will be scored on the carbon footprint of every dish they choose. Diners Sara Pascoe, Amol Rajan, Nikki Fox, Desiree Burch and Matthew Fort choose from a menu of tantalising treats, each of which tells its own environmental story

programme.title: The Noughties
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon is joined by singer Alexandra Burke and comedian Chris Ramsey to take a look at the year's highlights, including Barack Obama becoming America's first black president and Gwyneth Paltrow's launch of lifestyle brand Goop. Alexandra also reflects on winning The X Factor that year and how it felt to share a stage with Beyoncé

programme.title: Celebrity: A 21st Century Story
programme.desc: The world of celebrity has been transformed since the turn of the century, driven by some of the most dramatic technological and cultural developments in recent times. The first episode examines how the Beckhams changed the model of the celebrity power couple, while the rise of reality TV led to a gigantic influx of people gaining overnight fame, fed by a press that saw an easy source of material. The programme also examines how leaked sex tapes changed the nature of celebrity scandal

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Rebecca Ellis had always dreamt of a move to Australia, inspired by her grandfather's tales of a beautiful and friendly country. Partner Kylie and her four children wanted to support her, but fears of leaving loved ones and the kids' beloved father behind were foremost in their minds. Could a week in Brisbane help them come round to Rebecca's way of thinking? Three years since we last caught up with them, where do they call home - Australia or the UK?

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Proud farmers Jim and Stuart compete in one of the UK's oldest and most prestigious sheepdog trials at Longshaw - but the competition is strong in this part of the Peak District. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore four more treasured family heirlooms, tackling a musical wooden bench, a Victorian kaleidoscope, military items owned by a soldier who served in both world wars and a pair of Royal Crown Derby peacocks

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Rob Beckett, in which contestants choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves

programme.title: Coast and Country Auctions
programme.desc: A visit to Spalding Plant and Vegetable Auction in Lincolnshire, where as many as 1,000 bags and boxes of vegetables and up to 16,000 plants, shrubs and trees can be sold in a day

programme.title: Coast and Country Auctions
programme.desc: A visit to the Bala Autumn Sale in Snowdonia, north Wales, which is one of the UK's most important sheep dog auctions. Narrated by Stephen Tompkinson

programme.title: Made in Great Britain
programme.desc: Steph McGovern leads the makers to Wensleydale in the Yorkshire Dales, where they examine the area's famous cheese, as championed by Wallace and Gromit. Focusing on the Monastic period, Jason and Claire try to milk a sheep, while Katie and Charlton extract rennet - an enzyme vital in cheesemaking - from a sheep's stomach. Moving on to the Victorian era, Charlton and Jason tackle the highly physical task of collecting 20 litres of milk each in heavy back cans. The makers are then propelled to the 1920s when early factories employed equipment such as thermometers to produce cheese on a much larger scale

programme.title: Made in Great Britain
programme.desc: The makers explore how Northampton became the shoe capital of Britain, as Steph McGovern takes them through the ages, guided by master shoemaker Scott McKee, who has hand-made shoes for dozens of high profile clients including Prince Charles and Daniel Craig. The makers start by recreating a 13th century turn shoe, then become part of the Victorian-era production line for the heeled Oxford shoe. Other tasks include producing a Second World-War marching boot that kitted out the British army and working on modern leather brogue boots

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: In Muncaster, Cumbria, hundreds of people turn up with collectibles and treasures they want to take to auction. Antiques experts James Lewis and Caroline Hawley uncover a few gems, including a dazzling decanter and an art nouveau picture frame. Presenter Paul Martin also finds time to visit Coniston Water in the Lake District

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Gemma Cairney, Tim Key, Gabby Logan and Jeff Stelling test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games. Their scores are tallied across the week resulting in an overall champion

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick meets his good friend, the actor and comedian Barry Humphries, who fell in love with Cornwall in the 1960s when he escaped London to develop his famous character Dame Edna Everage. Barry talks about his friendship with British poet John Betjeman, and asks Rick to review his home-made fishcakes. Rick also goes fishing for crab, before cooking a simple yet delicious crab omelette. The host then takes a dip in the chilly north Atlantic with an eccentric group of wild water swimmers called the Perranporth Blue Tits

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: A look at the smaller boats that fish off Cornwall's coast, often with only a single crew member. Despite being a sustainable method of fishing, this traditional approach is coming under threat from industrialisation, a lack of new talent taking up the trade, and declining stocks of mackerel and other once common fish. In Penberth, James is the only fisherman under 65 - and since traditional methods rely on boats supporting each other, he may have to give up when the older generation retires. On the Helford River, a veteran fisherman shares his concerns for the fish processing industry if Britain leaving the EU leads to a decline in foreign labour

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Part one of two. Monty Don and his team of horticultural experts feature in this selection of highlights from the 2020 series

programme.title: The Wife (2017)
programme.desc: Successful writer Joe Castleman is delighted to receive an early morning telephone call from Sweden informing him that he has won this year's Nobel Prize for Literature. His steadfast wife Joan is equally delighted, but as she prepares to travel to Stockholm to support and encourage her husband, she reflects on her life from when she first met Joe, her married literature tutor with whom she embarks on the affair which would change the course of their lives. Oscar-nomiinated drama, starring Glenn Close, Jonathan Pryce and Max Irons

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul visits Coll, where he meets crofter Angus Kennedy, and also visits the owner of Breachacha Castle, Nicolas Maclean Bristol

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: A unique episode of the sci-fi drama set largely from the Cylon perspective. On Caprica, the murdered Sharon is downloaded into a new body and an insight into Cylon society is revealed on the occupied Twelve Colonies, while Anders continues his guerilla war. On Galactica, the captive incarnation of Sharon gives birth prematurely to the hybrid child, who soon becomes the focus of intrigue

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Part one of two. As Roslin and Baltar go head to head in a presidential debate, the discovery of a habitable planet helps to shift the popularity of one candidate dramatically. Meanwhile, Chief Tyrol suffers with terrible visions that prompt him to attack Cally and seek guidance from a priest, and Starbuck leads a rescue mission back to Cylon-occupied Caprica in the hope of finding Anders alive

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: The comedian and explorer revisits his fifth travel series - Himalaya. For Michael Palin, this epic journey across the world's highest mountain range is the fulfilment of boyhood dreams inspired by the 1953 ascent of Everest by Hillary and Tensing. Beginning in the famous Khyber Pass, he travels through Pakistan and India, ascends into Nepal and Tibet to reach Everest base camp itself

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: The team builds a 'plane den' for 11-year-old Philip, in the hope that a place where he can indulge his fascination with the RAF will help him cope with his digestive illness

programme.title: What's Cooking Omari?
programme.desc: Young cook Omari makes mega-loaded fries. These crunchy potato wedges topped with spicy flavours are the perfect snack to give the family all the energy they need to outwit one another during their game of mega hide-and-seek

programme.title: What's Cooking Omari?
programme.desc: Young cook Omari is creating his family's favourite snack - choccy oat bars. His yummy snack, filled with oats, syrup and chocolate, is the perfect treat

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Lindsey, Adam and Mwaksy meet some very special animals at the zoo

programme.title: DIY Deadly with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: From adders to anacondas, wildlife expert Steve Backshall answers questions about snakes

programme.title: DIY Deadly with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: From budgies to beetles, wildlife expert Steve Backshall answers questions about animals that live in and around people's backyards

programme.title: Operation Grand Canyon with Dan Snow
programme.desc: Part two of two. The historian and his team continue down the Colorado River in antique wooden boats as they re-create the pioneering 280-mile expedition through the Grand Canyon that was led by explorer John Wesley Powell in 1869. Along the way they carry out the same surveying tasks as the original party with period equipment, including taking photographs with a 19th-century 3D camera

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Mozart's situation improves after she is let back into the gang, but the Whiskers suffer a tragedy when they battle with the Commandos

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Flower's pups find themselves alone during a storm when their uncle Youssarian abandons his babysitting duties. Meanwhile, the Lazulis try to expand their territory and move a little too close for the Whiskers' liking

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: Dog fanatic Sheridan Smith and her fluffy co-host Stanley are on a mission to find the nation's best professional dog groomer. Heat one sees the first four contestants working on a long-haired shih-tzu - one of the most popular breeds to grace British salons. After that they let their imaginations fly as they give four curly haired canines a teddy bear trim to parade on the dogwalk in front of their owners

programme.title: Cheetah Family & Me
programme.desc: Part one of two. Wildlife film-maker Gordon Buchanan gets close to wild cheetah in the Kalahari, experiencing first-hand the very real dangers these beautiful big cats face. The programme follows two females, each with a litter of cubs, as they navigate a harsh environment, dangerous wildlife and dwindling prey

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers look across the Channel to see how the French have influenced British food over the centuries. They prepare a roast chicken with 40 cloves of garlic, and a delicate mille feuille, and examine UK products in traditionally French areas of gastronomy, from award-winning English wines to Scottish garlic

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Suppers
programme.desc: The food writer unravels the magic behind the combination of sugar and spice to reveal why this exotic marriage works so well in the kitchen

programme.title: Black Narcissus (1947)
programme.desc: British nuns establish a school and hospital on a remote mountainside in the Himalayas, where the nursing sisters face isolation as well as native mistrust. However, it is the presence of a male land agent that takes a final toll on their peace of mind. Oscar-winning drama from directorial team Powell and Pressburger. With Deborah Kerr, David Farrar, Kathleen Byron, Flora Robson and Jean Simmons

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Examining how animals, including monkeys, wolves, bears, foxes and birds, cope with mountain life in the Himalayas, and how the harsh terrain has given many creatures remarkable strength. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Members of the public gather at the Imperial War Museum Duxford in Cambridgeshire to present their antiques, and Paul Martin finds out more about the Hawker Hurricane, famed for its role in the Battle of Britain

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: Eight celebrities embark on an experimental adventure to Jaipur in India, to test whether they can set up a more rewarding retirement than in the UK. Wayne Sleep, Miriam Margolyes, Rosemary Shrager, Bobby George, Jan Leeming, Patti Boulaye, Roy Walker and Sylvester McCoy take over a private mansion in the heart of the old city and have to work out how to set up home together. In the opening episode, the group are shocked by the realities of everyday life in India, including the hectic roads, poor toilet facilities and animals wandering the streets

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: In the Solent, one the busiest stretches of water in the UK, reports come in of a missing kite surfer. The crew of Lymington lifeboat station start searching, but as the hours pass they begin to fear the worst. Twenty miles to the west, the Poole crew are called out to save a father and daughter from drowning as they are swept out to sea, and at Chiswick, one of the few RNLI stations with a crew on permanent standby 24/7, an unusual casualty in the Thames leaves the crew attempting to save not just one but nine lives

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Anneka Rice and Liz Carr head out on a treasure-hunting quest in the Midlands, joined by expert assistants Philip Serrell and James Braxton, before going to auction in Greenwich. Liz also learns how criminal investigations were brought into the modern age, while Anneka heads to a local farm - much to the bewilderment of the farmer

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: First-ever episode of the classic comedy about the Home Guard, starring Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier. Bank manager George Mainwaring responds to the call to set up a Local Defence Volunteers platoon in the town of Walmington-on-Sea on the south coast of England, assembling a motley crew of locals to protect Britain from Nazi invasion. First shown in 1968

programme.title: Mark Lawson Talks To
programme.desc: Another chance to see this interview with the best-selling author, who died last month. Famous for writing espionage thrillers, le Carre started as a real-life spy working for MI6, and wrote the first of his hugely popular books while in the secret service. In conversation with Mark Lawson, le Carre discusses his work and his books, including discusses his life and work the novels Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Spy and The Spy Who Came in from the Cold

programme.title: Tinker Tailor Soldier Spy (2011)
programme.desc: George Smiley, the deputy to the head of British intelligence, is forced into retirement after an operation ends in disaster with the death of an MI6 agent. Smiley is later recruited by the government to return to duty and investigate the possibility of a Soviet mole in a high-ranking position within MI6. Cold War thriller based on John le Carré's 1974 novel, starring an Oscar-nominated Gary Oldman, alongside Colin Firth, Tom Hardy, John Hurt, Benedict Cumberbatch, Toby Jones, Mark Strong, Ciaran Hinds and Roger Lloyd Pack

programme.title: The Big Short (2015)
programme.desc: Four investors each become aware of a flaw in the housing market that makes a financial crisis in the next few years inevitable. Each comes up with a plan to profit from the coming economic meltdown, but as their predictions come true, they are confronted with the devastating consequences for ordinary people. Fact-based drama, starring Christian Bale, Steve Carell, Ryan Gosling and Brad Pitt

programme.title: Apprentice (2016)
programme.desc: A soft-spoken corrections officer transferred to a high-security prison is taken under the wing of the facility's chief executioner. Drama, starring Firdaus Rahman, Wan Hanafi Su and Mastura Ahmad. In Malay and English

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Winter Walks
programme.desc: Yorkshire peer Baroness Sayeeda Warsi takes time out from her political life to seek peace and calm in the Dales. On her ramble through Wharfedale, the former government minister meets villagers in Kettlewell, a farmer preparing for lambing and a long-distance fell runner

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross recreates the fog of a snowy morning as the sun awakens, announcing the start of a new day

programme.title: Tea with Mussolini (1999)
programme.desc: Eccentric English and American women living in Florence during the tense pre-war years of the 1930s experience the ups and downs of parenthood when they collectively adopt a local boy disowned by his father. However, as international hostilities break out, their happy existence comes under threat. Period comedy drama, starring Maggie Smith, Judi Dench, Joan Plowright, Cher, Lily Tomlin and Baird Wallace

programme.title: Nothing Like a Dame
programme.desc: Eileen Atkins, Judi Dench, Joan Plowright and Maggie Smith let the cameras in on a friendship that goes back more than half a century. Coming together for a weekend at the country retreat Dame Joan once shared with her husband Laurence Olivier, the acting greats discuss their lives and careers, reminisce about their humble beginnings in the theatre, and reflect on love and loss

programme.title: Knights of Classic Drama at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive footage of some of Britain's most acclaimed acting legends appearing in BBC drama at early stages of their careers. The programme features early roles and some career-defining moments for Judi Dench, Maggie Smith, Diana Rigg and Helen Mirren. Narrated by David Tennant

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross recreates the fog of a snowy morning as the sun awakens, announcing the start of a new day

programme.title: Max Richter's Sleep (2019)
programme.desc: Cameras follow acclaimed composer and musician Max Richter and his creative partner, artist and film-maker Yulia Mahr, as they navigate an ambitious performance of his eight-hour opus Sleep at an open-air concert in Los Angeles

programme.title: Winter Walks
programme.desc: Yorkshire peer Baroness Sayeeda Warsi takes time out from her political life to seek peace and calm in the Dales. On her ramble through Wharfedale, the former government minister meets villagers in Kettlewell, a farmer preparing for lambing and a long-distance fell runner

programme.title: Murder 24/7
programme.desc: The investigation into the murder of Courtney Valentine-Brown nears its conclusion. With one suspect outstanding, one on bail and one in custody, can the team of detectives and specialists secure enough evidence to charge them with murder?

programme.title: Winter Walks
programme.desc: Celebrities explore landscapes in Yorkshire and Cumbria. The Rev Richard Coles walks through the North York Moors and along the Sutton Bank. His journey takes him to the ruins of Rievaulx Abbey, once one of England's most powerful Cistercian monasteries. where he reflects on the need for silent contemplation

programme.title: Sounds of the 70s 2
programme.desc: Collection of archive performances by artists including the Kinks, Roxy Music, Elton John, New York Dolls, Queen, Sparks and Rod Stewart. Plus, a recently rediscovered recording of David Bowie singing The Jean Genie from January 1973

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Mark Goodier hosts the edition first aired on June 7, with performances by artists including Pop Will Eat Itself, Don Pablo's Animals, Elton John, D-Shake, the Mission, Wilson Phillips, MC Tunes versus 808 State, Was (Not Was) and Roxette

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes hosts the edition first aired on June 14, with performances by artists including Guru Josh, Maureen Walsh, Snap, Maxi Priest, Bobby Brown, the Charlatans, Betty Boo, New Kids on the Block, England and New Order and 49ers

programme.title: David Bowie: The Last Five Years
programme.desc: A portrait of David Bowie, told by the people who knew him best - his friends and artistic collaborators. It also takes a detailed look at his last albums The Next Day and Blackstar, and his play Lazarus. Through the prism of this last work, the film shows how, in his final five years, Bowie not only began producing music again but returned to the core and defining themes of his career. Among the contributors are Tony Visconti, Gail Ann Dorsey, Toni Basil and Michael C Hall

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: Special edition of the archive hits programme dedicated to the singer, featuring performances from Top of the Pops and The Old Grey Whistle Test as well as archive interviews

programme.title: Bowie at the BBC
programme.desc: A chronology of clips from the BBC archive giving an overview of Bowie's career from 1964 until his death in 2016, offering a series of snapshots into his rapidly evolving career across music, films and the theatre. Among the many clips are performances for Top of the Pops, The Old Grey Whistle Test and Later with Jools Holland, plus Bowie in interviews about his roles in The Elephant Man, Merry Christmas Mr Lawrence and Labyrinth

programme.title: The Genius of David Bowie
programme.desc: A compilation showcasing some of the musician's performances from the BBC archives, including Space Oddity, Life on Mars?, Fashion and The Jean Genie. Plus, Lulu and Mott the Hoople perform Bowie songs, and there are further clips featuring the star's musical collaborators Iggy Pop and Lou Reed

programme.title: Sounds of the 70s 2
programme.desc: Collection of archive performances by artists including the Kinks, Roxy Music, Elton John, New York Dolls, Queen, Sparks and Rod Stewart. Plus, a recently rediscovered recording of David Bowie singing The Jean Genie from January 1973

programme.title: Simon King's Shetland Diaries
programme.desc: Spring brings an influx of wildlife to the islands, where the former Springwatch presenter is living for a year. Exploring the sea, Simon discovers starfish, moon jellyfish and gets close to a seal. He also follows the struggle at a wildlife sanctuary to teach an injured otter cub to use its back legs, and gives his daughter Savannah a lesson in birdwatching

programme.title: Sahara with Michael Palin
programme.desc: The former Monty Python star's expedition takes him to Senegal, where he witnesses a wrestling competition, visits jazz clubs, and meets soap star Marie-Madeleine. He also visits Bamako and Dogon country, then travels to Djenne, where he celebrates the Muslim Tabaski feast and secures a passage on a cargo boat to take him down the Niger River to Timbuktu

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Gilou is offered a way out of jail if he agrees to become a police informer, but on the condition that he keeps away from Laure. Meanwhile, the police team focus their investigation on the Paris neighbourhood of Barbès, where vulnerable delinquent minors are being exploited by receivers of stolen goods. In French

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: The police investigation screeches to a sudden halt when Souleymane disappears. How and where will they find him? Meanwhile, Laure flouts the ban on seeing Gilou, who has got himself hired as a bouncer in Cisco's nightclub. There he meets the security manager, Cisco's son Titi. In French

programme.title: Paris
programme.desc: Art historian Sandrine Voillet presents a guide to Paris, celebrating 800 years of its arts, design and architecture. She begins by visiting the Louvre, a fairy-tale park and the city's sewers, before heading to a restaurant known for its boudoir lounges. Sandrine also meets French photographer Willy Ronis and reveals the controversy surrounding the construction of the Eiffel Tower and the Basilica of Sacre Coeur

programme.title: Mimi & Co
programme.desc: In the last episode of the series, Mimi enlists her brother Leo’s help to fulfil a lifelong dream: to win a Blue Peter badge. She teaches Leo to rollerblade, they upcycle a mirror together and draw portraits of each other, only to discover they are too old to apply for a badge! We meet proud badge holders Iris and Aidan, and find out all about the Chinese Mid- Autumn Festival, which they celebrate with their mum and dad. Luckily for Mimi, Blue Peter’s very own Mwaksy invites Mimi to co-host Fan Club Live, and there is a special surprise for her in the post.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters are going to Westman Islands, Iceland, where the world’s largest colony of Atlantic puffins gathers every year. Lars loves these tough little birds and explains how, although they spend most of their lives flying out to sea and fishing, they return to land each year to lay their eggs. When the baby birds, also called pufflings, eventually hatch, they fly out towards the ocean. But the light from nearby towns often confuses them about where to go, leading local children and adults to form 'puffin patrols' to rescue the pufflings. Lars is excited to help out but is upset when he discovers Glitch, also a fan of puffins, is already well-known on the islands for his skills as a puffin rescuer. Although Glitch offers him a place in his puffin patrol, Lars is determined to outdo Glitch with a patrol of his own - but when he recruits a clumsy Grimbot, things take a turn for the worse...

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: But even though it's sunny when they leave the house, a rainy weather forecast threatens her plans. Luckily for her, Gran Gran has come prepared!

Partner Kylie and her four children wanted to support her, but fears of leaving loved ones and the kids' beloved father behind were foremost in their minds. Could a week in Brisbane help them come round to Rebecca's way of thinking? Three years since we last caught up with them, where do they call home - Australia or the UK?
