programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Patrick's good intentions catch up with him, leaving Denise furious and him heartbroken. Ruby makes a snap admission in an attempt to keep Martin close, while Martin is inspired by Stacey's strength. Gray tells Karen that he wants his kids back for good

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: Dog fanatic Sheridan Smith and her fluffy co-host Stanley are on a mission to find the nation's best professional dog groomer. Heat one sees the first four contestants working on a long-haired shih-tzu - one of the most popular breeds to grace British salons. After that they let their imaginations fly as they give four curly haired canines a teddy bear trim to parade on the dogwalk in front of their owners

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Caribbean-set whodunit returns for its 10th anniversary series, which promises more murders and the return of one or two familiar faces. A TV news presenter is found dead in her pool at home. DI Neville Parker is convinced her co-host is responsible. But given he was live on air at the time, how could he have done it? The return of an experienced sergeant to the island could be just what Neville needs to crack the case

programme.title: The Limehouse Golem (2016)
programme.desc: After a series of violent murders scandalises Victorian London, the media-dubbed Limehouse Golem strikes fear into the hearts of the public. Into this melting pot is thrust well-intentioned inspector John Kildare, who has never been given a murder case before. The Scotland Yard sleuth knows he has been set up to fail, but soon uncovers a lead that implicates Karl Marx, George Gissing and the popular music hall star Dan Leno. He follows the trail, wherever it may lead, through the seediest and most intriguing parts of London. Thriller, starring Bill Nighy, Douglas Booth and Olivia Cooke

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: The music fans stuck with tickets for concerts abroad that were cancelled due to coronavirus, and the static caravan owners whose holiday havens are headed for the junkyard

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell and Martin Roberts follow the progress of terraced houses in Frizington in Cumbria and Wolverhampton and a huge commercial building in Stoke-on-Trent

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A plastic bag heat sealer that convicted an online drug trafficker. Plus, a casual chat with a criminal and a map join the dots to catch three burglars terrorising a neighbourhood

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion goes upstairs and downstairs looking back at the buys that best reveal Britain's class divide. The show compares items used by the wealthy and those working below stairs and also visits a workhouse to look at those who turned their back on domestic service

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Daily Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Mallory and Father Brown go missing, and it is up to the gang to save them from a man hell-bent on revenge. Mark Williams and Jack Deam star in the crime drama

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers prepare dishes to feed a multitude. Their salmon pie with spinach and hollandaise is a showstopper, while their chocolate and cherry buns make a perfect centrepiece

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: After losing her father, Cheryl Castling was determined to live life to the fullest, and thought that a move to Oz could be the adventure she and her husband Adam needed. Adam was less than convinced, despite being the one to have the idea in the first place. Could a week of living the Aussie life make him see where Cheryl was coming from? Four years on, have they been able to come to a decision about where home should be? Presented by Nicki Chapman

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Husband-and-wife cider-makers Liz and Mike are excited to bring their cider, made from eight varieties of apples, to the South of England Agricultural Show. Meanwhile, Andy, his wife Anna and their two young children Lillie and Daisy have spent the last few days gearing up for the show's poultry competition. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Deep in the Yorkshire countryside, Nigel Havers welcomes five people with intriguing pieces they are hoping to sell. To help them before they face the dealers, Nigel introduces them to resident valuation expert Simon, who has been an auctioneer for nearly 30 years. Dealers then bid for a lamp, Victorian wall mirror, Black Forest inkwell and a quirky sideboard

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants have to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Harry Judd host the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Dirty Vegan
programme.desc: Dedicated vegan Mathew Pritchard feels the pressure as he designs and cooks a vegan spread for 160 wedding guests at a country estate house in south Wales. It's the bride and groom's special day and Matt must hit their exacting specifications, the standards of the venue and the expectations of the guests. With a cake and a selection of signature dishes to prepare, this tricky catering challenge has Matt in a spin

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Martin gives Sonia some words of encouragement as they share their problems. Ben finds himself in a tricky situation at the police station. Linda hopes she has made the right decision and Denise is in for another shock - but is everything as it seems?

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell are joined by former Premier League midfielder Steve Sidwell, Winter Olympic snowboarder Aimee Fuller, 2019 World Indoor champion sprinter Richard Kilty, and netball star Pamela Cookey. Sue Barker asks the questions

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Return of the comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Taking part in this first edition are Les Dennis, Richard Osman, Alice Levine and Lou Sanders

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Social niceties are stretched to snapping point when Lee and Lucy take in a neighbour's delivery for safekeeping. Lee unknowingly pulls apart the packaging, only to discover it's a somewhat personal item. To top it off, there's no way to hide the fact it's been opened. Thus, getting it back to its rightful owner proves easier said than done. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton, with Hugh Dennis

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Peep Show duo David Mitchell and Robert Webb, who have reunited for a new series of Back. Oscar-winner Regina King discusses making her directing debut with One Night in Miami, while actor Anya Taylor-Joy talks about hit chess drama The Queen's Gambit. Singer Olly Alexander reflects on starring in the new Russell T Davies series It's a Sin, and US comedian Jimmy Fallon, host of The Tonight Show. Plus, '; comedian Mel Giedroyc promotes new panel show Unforgivable and singer-songwriter Yungblud performs the single Cotton Candy

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Meat and dairy lover Hayley Pearce looks at veganism in a bid to understand what the growing trend is all about. From farmers and footballers to activists and models, Hayley meets people on all sides of the debate. Exploring the world of diets, fitness and animal welfare, Hayley wants to see if a plant-based lifestyle could work for her. She finds a novel way to get her much-needed cheese fix and braces herself for a visit to an abattoir. At the end of the two weeks, will she give up her carnivore lifestyle or will the lure of a Saturday night kebab prove just too much?

programme.title: War of the Worlds (2005)
programme.desc: A divorced father's rare weekend with his two estranged children descends into horror when alien war machines rise from beneath Earth and begin to systematically destroy every human being in their path - leaving the man with the dangerous task of getting his kids to safety. Steven Spielberg's sci-fi thriller, based on HG Wells' novel, with Tom Cruise, Dakota Fanning, Justin Chatwin, Miranda Otto and Tim Robbins

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: National and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45. Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Adam Byatt and Anna Haugh, and guest Tamzin Outhwaite, while drinks expert Olly Smith selects the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: The chef reveals the Cornwall that he knows and loves, championing the food, history, music, art and culture of the county,. He begins at his family home in Trevose Head on the north coast, where as a child he would go fishing with his father

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to what is one of the most eagerly anticipated weekends of the football calendar, when the FA Cup third round takes place

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross challenges two teams in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, to scour two antique centres and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction in Norfolk. Experts Jonathan Pratt and Colin Young offer support, while away from the action Charlie sees a selection of of rare toys

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley meets a couple who are looking to lay down roots in Devon after decades serving in the Army, with a £400,000 budget to find their ideal home near the English Riviera. Away from the property hunt, Ginny capitalises on being in Devon at the same time as the County Show to find out what it takes to win Pig of the Year

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades scours the recycling centre in Altrincham, Greater Manchester, in search of three skip-bound castoffs he can rework and repurpose. Woodworking specialist Bruce Faulseit is on hand to give an old bookcase a new look, while upholsterer Ray Clarke attempts a creative makeover on a set of dining chairs. For his own project, Jay has a plant stand to spruce up

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the FA Cup, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Arsenal v Newcastle United (Kick-off 5.30pm). Coverage of the third-round fixture, which takes place at Emirates Stadium, as the holders take on Steve Bruce's Magpies. The Gunners have struggled badly in the Premier League so far this season, and may see this competition as a welcome distraction. The sides last met in this tournament at the fourth-round stage in 2008, when an Emmanuel Adebayor double and a Nicky Butt own goal sealed a routine 3-0 victory for Arsene Wenger's team

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jacob wants more from Connie, but she is unsure if will she be able to make the time for their relationship with mounting pressures on the emergency department. Meanwhile, Faith confronts Lev about his secret, and new paramedic Leon arrives to meet the rest of the team and complete a Covid-19 training course

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: Award-winning comedian Michael McIntyre hosts, as contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing the cash prize

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The comedian has never been very good at DIY, finding even the simple task of hanging a picture invariably ending in disaster. So it remains to be seen how he will cope after turning up at one of Wales's biggest building sites and being expected to get stuck in. From bricklaying to plumbing and carpentry, Rhod has to go all out to prove he has what it takes to become a master builder

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Action from the third round, including Manchester United v Watford at OId Trafford, Aston Villa v Liverpool at Villa Park and Boreham Wood v Millwall at Meadow Park. Plus, all the goals and major talking points from the day's other fixtures, including Everton v Rotherham United, Chorley v Derby County, Exeter City v Sheffield Wednesday, Queens Park Rangers v Fulham, Southampton v Shrewsbury Town and Burnley v Milton Keynes Dons

programme.title: The NFL Show

programme.title: End of Watch (2012)
programme.desc: Two Los Angeles cops make their regular patrol of the city, dealing with petty offences while one records their lives on camera for a documentary project. The officers end up in over their heads when they stumble on evidence that could bring down a drug cartel, putting them squarely in the gang's firing line. Crime thriller, with Jake Gyllenhaal and Michael Pena

programme.title: The Limehouse Golem (2016)
programme.desc: After a series of violent murders scandalises Victorian London, the media-dubbed Limehouse Golem strikes fear into the hearts of the public. Into this melting pot is thrust well-intentioned inspector John Kildare, who has never been given a murder case before. The Scotland Yard sleuth knows he has been set up to fail, but soon uncovers a lead that implicates Karl Marx, George Gissing and the popular music hall star Dan Leno. He follows the trail, wherever it may lead, through the seediest and most intriguing parts of London. Thriller, starring Bill Nighy, Douglas Booth and Olivia Cooke

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A plastic bag heat sealer that convicted an online drug trafficker. Plus, a casual chat with a criminal and a map join the dots to catch three burglars terrorising a neighbourhood

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion goes upstairs and downstairs looking back at the buys that best reveal Britain's class divide. The show compares items used by the wealthy and those working below stairs and also visits a workhouse to look at those who turned their back on domestic service

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Mallory and Father Brown go missing, and it is up to the gang to save them from a man hell-bent on revenge. Mark Williams and Jack Deam star in the crime drama

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers prepare dishes to feed a multitude. Their salmon pie with spinach and hollandaise is a showstopper, while their chocolate and cherry buns make a perfect centrepiece

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah is on a mission to help find a country home for £300,000 in the East Midlands for an eager buyer who has brought along her friendly neighbour for support. While in the region, Sonali pays a visit to an Edwardian house in Worksop, now managed by the National Trust, and steps back in time to learn about everyday life and work in the region during that era

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Husband-and-wife cider-makers Liz and Mike are excited to bring their cider, made from eight varieties of apples, to the South of England Agricultural Show. Meanwhile, Andy, his wife Anna and their two young children Lillie and Daisy have spent the last few days gearing up for the show's poultry competition. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Deep in the Yorkshire countryside, Nigel Havers welcomes five people with intriguing pieces they are hoping to sell. To help them before they face the dealers, Nigel introduces them to resident valuation expert Simon, who has been an auctioneer for nearly 30 years. Dealers then bid for a lamp, Victorian wall mirror, Black Forest inkwell and a quirky sideboard

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants have to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: Reporting Scotland; Weather
programme.desc: Presented by Sally Magnusson

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Harry Judd host the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Scotland's Home of the Year
programme.desc: In Argyll and the west of Scotland, judges Michael Angus, Anna Campbell Jones and Kate Spiers visit a waterside abode in Oban. Designed as the owners' 'forever home', it demonstrates some clever design inside - and some stunning views outside. They then head to a personality-packed home on the Isle of Bute decorated with skulls, swords and a sculptured wooden horse, and a glass-fronted seaside home in West Kilbride, with stunning views across to Arran

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Martin gives Sonia some words of encouragement as they share their problems. Ben finds himself in a tricky situation at the police station. Linda hopes she has made the right decision and Denise is in for another shock - but is everything as it seems?

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell are joined by former Premier League midfielder Steve Sidwell, Winter Olympic snowboarder Aimee Fuller, 2019 World Indoor champion sprinter Richard Kilty, and netball star Pamela Cookey. Sue Barker asks the questions

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Return of the comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Taking part in this first edition are Les Dennis, Richard Osman, Alice Levine and Lou Sanders

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Social niceties are stretched to snapping point when Lee and Lucy take in a neighbour's delivery for safekeeping. Lee unknowingly pulls apart the packaging, only to discover it's a somewhat personal item. To top it off, there's no way to hide the fact it's been opened. Thus, getting it back to its rightful owner proves easier said than done. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton, with Hugh Dennis

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Peep Show duo David Mitchell and Robert Webb, who have reunited for a new series of Back. Oscar-winner Regina King discusses making her directing debut with One Night in Miami, while actor Anya Taylor-Joy talks about hit chess drama The Queen's Gambit. Singer Olly Alexander reflects on starring in the new Russell T Davies series It's a Sin, and US comedian Jimmy Fallon, host of The Tonight Show. Plus, '; comedian Mel Giedroyc promotes new panel show Unforgivable and singer-songwriter Yungblud performs the single Cotton Candy

programme.title: War of the Worlds (2005)
programme.desc: A divorced father's rare weekend with his two estranged children descends into horror when alien war machines rise from beneath Earth and begin to systematically destroy every human being in their path - leaving the man with the dangerous task of getting his kids to safety. Steven Spielberg's sci-fi thriller, based on HG Wells' novel, with Tom Cruise, Dakota Fanning, Justin Chatwin, Miranda Otto and Tim Robbins

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: National and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45. Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Adam Byatt and Anna Haugh, and guest Tamzin Outhwaite, while drinks expert Olly Smith selects the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: The chef reveals the Cornwall that he knows and loves, championing the food, history, music, art and culture of the county,. He begins at his family home in Trevose Head on the north coast, where as a child he would go fishing with his father

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to what is one of the most eagerly anticipated weekends of the football calendar, when the FA Cup third round takes place

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross challenges two teams in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, to scour two antique centres and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction in Norfolk. Experts Jonathan Pratt and Colin Young offer support, while away from the action Charlie sees a selection of of rare toys

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley meets a couple who are looking to lay down roots in Devon after decades serving in the Army, with a £400,000 budget to find their ideal home near the English Riviera. Away from the property hunt, Ginny capitalises on being in Devon at the same time as the County Show to find out what it takes to win Pig of the Year

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades scours the recycling centre in Altrincham, Greater Manchester, in search of three skip-bound castoffs he can rework and repurpose. Woodworking specialist Bruce Faulseit is on hand to give an old bookcase a new look, while upholsterer Ray Clarke attempts a creative makeover on a set of dining chairs. For his own project, Jay has a plant stand to spruce up

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned, then talk to the buyers about their plans for the buildings

programme.title: Sportscene Results
programme.desc: David Currie presents news and updates from today's matches in the Scottish Premiership and Scottish Cup

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Arsenal v Newcastle United (Kick-off 5.30pm). Coverage of the third-round fixture, which takes place at Emirates Stadium, as the holders take on Steve Bruce's Magpies. The Gunners have struggled badly in the Premier League so far this season, and may see this competition as a welcome distraction. The sides last met in this tournament at the fourth-round stage in 2008, when an Emmanuel Adebayor double and a Nicky Butt own goal sealed a routine 3-0 victory for Arsene Wenger's team

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jacob wants more from Connie, but she is unsure if will she be able to make the time for their relationship with mounting pressures on the emergency department. Meanwhile, Faith confronts Lev about his secret, and new paramedic Leon arrives to meet the rest of the team and complete a Covid-19 training course

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: Award-winning comedian Michael McIntyre hosts, as contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing the cash prize

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: When a handsome man appears in Foley's Pub, everyone presumes he must have set his attentions on Cathy. However, the stranger reveals he only has eyes for one very special woman - none other than Agnes Brown. Will the doting mum-of-six agree to go on her first date in donkey's years? And if she does, who is in for the bigger surprise - Agnes or the stranger? Festive instalment of the comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll, with Dermot O'Neill, Danny O'Carroll and Jennifer Gibney

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Action from the third round, including Manchester United v Watford at OId Trafford, Aston Villa v Liverpool at Villa Park and Boreham Wood v Millwall at Meadow Park. Plus, all the goals and major talking points from the day's other fixtures, including Everton v Rotherham United, Chorley v Derby County, Exeter City v Sheffield Wednesday, Queens Park Rangers v Fulham, Southampton v Shrewsbury Town and Burnley v Milton Keynes Dons

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present a preview of the Wild Card play-off games, as the post-season begins

programme.title: End of Watch (2012)
programme.desc: Two Los Angeles cops make their regular patrol of the city, dealing with petty offences while one records their lives on camera for a documentary project. The officers end up in over their heads when they stumble on evidence that could bring down a drug cartel, putting them squarely in the gang's firing line. Crime thriller, with Jake Gyllenhaal and Michael Pena

programme.title: Lennon Remembered: A Reporter's Journal
programme.desc: Tom Brook marks the fortieth anniversary of former Beatle John Lennon's murder in New York on 8 December 1980. His untimely death was a slaying that shocked millions of fans around the world. In Lennon Remembered: A Reporter's Journal, the BBC's Tom Brook, the first British journalist to report live from the site of the New York shooting, takes stock of Lennon's life and the legacy of his music. Brook recounts what happened on the night Lennon died when he returned to his home, the Dakota apartment building on Manhattan's Upper West Side, with his wife Yoko Ono, and he hears from some of Lennon's closest confidantes as well as younger Lennon fans in America today. Drawing on a wealth of archive material, the programme includes Tom Brook's interview with the former Beatle's widow Yoko Ono, one of the first she gave, in which she discussed Lennon's life and death. The programme assesses John Lennon's powerful legacy 40 years on and asks whether his messages of love and peace are still re

programme.title: Highlands: Scotland's Wild Heart
programme.desc: The course of the seasons, beginning with spring, which sees the hills on fire on fire as the annual muirburn takes place in the Cairngorms. As the flames rise, an osprey returns to a valley near Loch Ness, with its movement captured in super slow-motion at 800 frames per second. Red squirrels and roe deer prepare for the breeding season, while a cataclysmic downpour has major consequences for a family of dippers, whose youngsters are forced to fledge early before the nest is swept away. Narrated by Ewan McGregor

programme.desc: Dundee United v St Johnstone. Full-match coverage from Tannadice. With commentary by Alex O'Henley

programme.desc: Highlights from the music show, including a flavour of live performance from Travis at the the QMU in Glasgow, plus chat from Fran Healey and Dougie Payne from the band

programme.title: Alba Today

programme.title: Tipping Point

programme.title: Emmerdale

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: A year ago Tonight reported on how quickly Britain might be heading towards a cash-free society. Twelve months on and the world of money has changed dramatically with lockdowns and the pandemic accelerating the shift away from coins and notes. Some are now warning that the entire cash system, costing £5billion a year to run, could be under threat. Adnan Sarwar investigates

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sarah makes a bad decision, Chas calls the police, and Charity is uneasy

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show: Live
programme.desc: Martin returns after the festive break with his bill-busting tips for January. He answers questions on how the UK's relationship with Europe could be impacting on family finances, and there's also the latest News You Can Use and Martin's Big Briefing

programme.title: Beat the Chasers
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts as more people from across the UK are invited to play against arguably the most formidable quiz team on TV. First up is a cash builder of up to five questions, after which they have the option of playing between two, three, four or five of the Chasers for increasing amounts of money - and those brave enough to take on the Beast, the Governess, the Sinnerman, the Vixen and the Dark Destroyer could walk away with a huge cash prize

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Unbelievable Moments Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong presents a selection of extraordinary camera footage, including a climber in Colorado who took a 40ft fall - with every terrifying moment captured on his bodycam - and a Northern Ireland family whose holiday to Greece was ruined by a freak storm. Also, the story of a paralysed man who set a world record by walking a marathon, a Transport for London employee who came up with a knockout idea to liven up his station announcements and a Bristol couple who tried to free a trapped turtle in Oman

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase

programme.desc: Simon finds Leanne unconscious in the flat, Michael vows to be a part of his child's life, and David and Shona discover their new address

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport. This time, staff at the airport deal with the aftermath of a polar vortex. programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Simon makes Leanne's wellbeing his priority, Imran pleads for leniency at Johnny's sentencing, and Dev is shocked at Asha's academic struggles

programme.title: Beat the Chasers
programme.desc: Contestants from across the UK take on Jenny 'The Vixen' Ryan, Anne 'The Governess' Hegerty, Shaun 'The Dark Destroyer' Wallace, Mark 'The Beast' Labbett and Paul 'The Sinnerman' Sinha, hoping to win big money. Bradley Walsh hosts

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Jack Reacher (2012)
programme.desc: A former army sniper is arrested following a random shooting that leaves five people dead, but protests his innocence. An elusive former military police investigator is called in to examine the case, and becomes convinced a conspiracy is at work. Action thriller based on Lee Child's series of novels, starring Tom Cruise, Rosamund Pike, Werner Herzog and Robert Duvall

programme.title: Beat the Chasers
programme.desc: Contestants from across the UK take on Jenny 'The Vixen' Ryan, Anne 'The Governess' Hegerty, Shaun 'The Dark Destroyer' Wallace, Mark 'The Beast' Labbett and Paul 'The Sinnerman' Sinha, hoping to win big money. Bradley Walsh hosts

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Now Murtaugh has met his match in the shape of former international CIA agent Wesley Cole, the newly melded partners attempt to find a steady rhythm as they work to solve the murder of a finance executive. Cole, who has returned home to assumes duty as an LAPD officer, also has to navigate his role with his ex and his daughter. Meanwhile, Avery grapples with the outcome of his campaign for city council. Damon Wayans, Seann William Scott and Kevin Rahm star in the fast-paced detective drama based on the hit feature films

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz and Weasel plan to enter Fizz's new condiment in a culinary contest. Magoo mistakes them for members of his band ready for a reunion.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks he's on a trip to Translovenia and takes shelter in a mansion owned by a vampire. In reality, this vampire is just Weasel.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: A bear has escaped from the zoo and Fizz is hell-bent on making it his pet. Magoo mistakes the bear for his niece who's come to spend the day with him.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: A duplicitous supervisor on board an oil rig tries to sabotage the facility's operation, and it is up to Lady Penelope and Kayo to stop it from sinking. Animated adventure, featuring the guest voice of Emilia Clarke (Game of Thrones), with Rosamund Pike and Angel Coulby

programme.title: Dare Master at Home
programme.desc: Baked beans and chopsticks, a bottle top challenge and 30 seconds to get 150 steps on a pedometer. Who will win and who faces a messy forfeit!

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mr. Bean becomes Mrs. Wicket's house-mate when he suffers a flea infestation. Will they get on?

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mr. Bean is determined to make the best cup of java, ever. Oh, and to get rich brewing it.

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.desc: Madcap slapstick and mayhem as the celebrated cat and mouse plot against each other

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows 10-year-old Craig Williams and his two friends, Kelsey and John Paul, as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek which acts as a utopia where children reign

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda: The Paws of Destiny
programme.desc: Feeling a little bored, Po and the kids are excited to accept an important invitation to the Forbidden City.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The teenage superheroes fight to save the world while getting up to mischievous antics

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined by guest Jenni Falconer and chefs Atul Kochhar and Peter Lloyd, who serve up prawn and monkfish curry, and Indonesian fish

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode shares a masterclass in making the perfect tuna steak served with a gremolata, while Lisa Faulkner is serving up comfort food with her oven-roasted chicken noodle soup

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: The presenter visits North Wales, and is joined by fellow culinary expert Stephen Terry. Their first stop is the grand Pale Hall, where head chef Gareth Stevenson is cooking Moroccan-inspired Welsh lamb

programme.title: Ainsley's Caribbean Kitchen
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott explores Trinidad with local chef Debra Sardinha as his guide, visiting a Hindu temple built on land reclaimed from the sea. Ainsley explores Port of Spain's market in search of ingredients to make grilled tamarind tofu with callaloo pesto

programme.title: South Africa with Gregg Wallace
programme.desc: The MasterChef presenter explores the country's most famous and best-loved landscapes and experiences, beginning by going on safari at the Amakhala Game Reserve in the Eastern Cape. After a drive through the reserve, he camps out overnight and tastes his first poike - a traditional South African stew - cooked over a campfire. In the morning, Gregg joins vet Will Fowlds and his team as they care for a wild leopard

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps, cute kids and practical jokes, including a car bonnet flying open on a motorway, a teenager terrified by a seagull and a toddler catching out her family with a classic gag - fake poo

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants David, Maureen, Luke and Alison pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity special of the classic game show, with Richard Ayoade, Katie McGlynn and Joe Swash all trying to say what they see, as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen charity

programme.title: The Masked Singer
programme.desc: Disco queen Sophie Ellis-Bextor and Spice Girl Mel B were the first two secret singers revealed in weeks one and two respectively. Now, another famous face is unmasked and eliminated - but not before Swan, Badger, Robin, Dragon and Sausage have taken to the stage to perform. Presented by Joel Dommett, with panellists Rita Ora, Jonathan Ross, Davina McCall and Mo Gilligan

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the spinning-chair singing contest, with Anne-Marie joining the coaching panel alongside old hands Olly Murs, Tom Jones and Will.i.am. The four coaches will once again be selecting their teams through blind auditions, where looks play no part in the selection process and each singer is chosen on vocal ability alone

programme.title: Paul Sinha's TV Showdown
programme.desc: Chaser, stand-up comedian and TV trivia genius Paul Sinha hosts a TV-themed comedy quiz show, joined by team captains Fay Ripley and Rob Beckett. Taking part in this first edition are Denise van Outen, Adil Ray, Catherine Tyldesley and Josh Widdicombe, who all compete to test their TV knowledge

programme.title: Rush Hour 2 (2001)
programme.desc: The two mismatched detectives travel to Hong Kong when terrorists bomb the US embassy, and are soon involved in keeping tabs on the prime suspects - high-ranking Triad members involved in an international counterfeiting ring. Action comedy sequel, starring Jackie Chan, Chris Tucker, John Lone, Zhang Ziyi, Roselyn Sanchez and Harris Yulin

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: A short bulletin rounding up the day's stories

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts a revival of the supermarket-based game show in which three pairs of shoppers go wild in the aisles to win a £3,000 prize

programme.desc: The presenter visits North Wales, and is joined by fellow culinary expert Stephen Terry. Their first stop is the grand Pale Hall, where head chef Gareth Stevenson is cooking Moroccan-inspired Welsh lamb

programme.title: Ainsley's Caribbean Kitchen
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott explores Trinidad with local chef Debra Sardinha as his guide, visiting a Hindu temple built on land reclaimed from the sea. Ainsley explores Port of Spain's market in search of ingredients to make grilled tamarind tofu with callaloo pesto

programme.title: South Africa with Gregg Wallace
programme.desc: The MasterChef presenter explores the country's most famous and best-loved landscapes and experiences, beginning by going on safari at the Amakhala Game Reserve in the Eastern Cape. After a drive through the reserve, he camps out overnight and tastes his first poike - a traditional South African stew - cooked over a campfire. In the morning, Gregg joins vet Will Fowlds and his team as they care for a wild leopard

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps, cute kids and practical jokes, including a car bonnet flying open on a motorway, a teenager terrified by a seagull and a toddler catching out her family with a classic gag - fake poo

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants David, Maureen, Luke and Alison pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity special of the classic game show, with Richard Ayoade, Katie McGlynn and Joe Swash all trying to say what they see, as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen charity

programme.title: The Masked Singer
programme.desc: Disco queen Sophie Ellis-Bextor and Spice Girl Mel B were the first two secret singers revealed in weeks one and two respectively. Now, another famous face is unmasked and eliminated - but not before Swan, Badger, Robin, Dragon and Sausage have taken to the stage to perform. Presented by Joel Dommett, with panellists Rita Ora, Jonathan Ross, Davina McCall and Mo Gilligan

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the spinning-chair singing contest, with Anne-Marie joining the coaching panel alongside old hands Olly Murs, Tom Jones and Will.i.am. The four coaches will once again be selecting their teams through blind auditions, where looks play no part in the selection process and each singer is chosen on vocal ability alone

programme.title: Paul Sinha's TV Showdown
programme.desc: Chaser, stand-up comedian and TV trivia genius Paul Sinha hosts a TV-themed comedy quiz show, joined by team captains Fay Ripley and Rob Beckett. Taking part in this first edition are Denise van Outen, Adil Ray, Catherine Tyldesley and Josh Widdicombe, who all compete to test their TV knowledge

programme.title: Rush Hour 2 (2001)
programme.desc: The two mismatched detectives travel to Hong Kong when terrorists bomb the US embassy, and are soon involved in keeping tabs on the prime suspects - high-ranking Triad members involved in an international counterfeiting ring. Action comedy sequel, starring Jackie Chan, Chris Tucker, John Lone, Zhang Ziyi, Roselyn Sanchez and Harris Yulin

programme.desc: A short bulletin rounding up the day's stories

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts a revival of the supermarket-based game show in which three pairs of shoppers go wild in the aisles to win a £3,000 prize

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois is not looking forward to her birthday, so she decides to turn her life around by adopting a different, younger lifestyle, growing her hair and having a makeover on a shopping trip. Meanwhile, Stewie finds a turtle at the park and adopts him as a new pet

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg reveals her infatuation with a popular boy at school, Kent Lastname. Esther suggests that she ask him out, but she fears almost certain rejection. Meanwhile, Stewie overhears the hunk mentioning Meg's brother and begins to think he might be gay

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Steve and his friends start up a live-action role-playing game, and Francine finds herself strangely drawn to the fantasy world they create. Meanwhile, Stan opens a marine animal theme park in the Smith family home

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan begins to question Christianity after Steve asks some uncomfortable questions about everything written in the Bible. Now facing a crisis of faith, the secret agent takes his family to South Korea to investigate reports of reinvented Noah's Ark

programme.title: The Cleveland Show

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Junior gets a new girlfriend, but Cleveland becomes jealous of their relationship - and suspects the young woman is only trying to seduce his son so she can get permission to stay in the US. Animated comedy, with the guest voice of Rosie Perez (White Men Can't Jump)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates another bunch of domestic mishaps, captured in video form by viewers. This time, a Brexit victory dance ends in disaster, a runaway car demolishes a wall, and a dog eats Lady Gaga

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring a child who is 'squashed' by a wardrobe, a teenager falling off a cliff, and a paraglider feeling sick during a selfie

programme.title: The Cabins
programme.desc: Fires are stoked and hot tubs are steaming down at the cabins, but one singleton is scrabbling for forgiveness in this reality dating series. After knocking up a monster roast dinner together, some serious burn sees a karaoke session turn into a party for one. It remains to be seen whether love can conquer this musical misery

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Quagmire accidentally marries a prostitute when he, Peter and Joe get drunk while visiting the Harvard University campus. Desperate to escape his new wife, he decides to pretend he is gay, and turns to one of his companions to become his emergency boyfriend

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and Lois send Chris to space camp to boost his self-esteem. When his stint ends and the Griffins arrive to pick him up, they are accidentally launched into space and it is down to the awkward youth to land the clan safely back on Earth

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian discovers that Carter Pewterschmidt's pharmaceutical company is withholding the cure for cancer from the public to boost its profits. He joins forces with Stewie and plots to reveal the truth, which could change the world for ever. Meanwhile, Quagmire reveals a hair-raising secret of his own

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan resolves to spend more father-son time with Steve, and the pair start bowling together, a pass-time at which the awkward teen proves to be quite gifted

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan hopes to avoid conducting a suicide mission by beating Bullock in a DJ battle, and Jeff dreams up a new musical instrument after drinking psychedelic tea

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Donna and Cleveland Jr compete in a dance contest - but just as they are set to take to the floor, the youngster makes an embarrassing admission that threatens their chances. Meanwhile, Roberta receives a harsh lesson about being a grown-up as she takes care of Rallo for a week

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland decides to form a ventriloquism act, but does not have a dummy and hits on the bright idea of using a thinly disguised Rallo - whose quick-witted comments earn them rave reviews. Unfortunately, their success soon leads to friction

programme.title: Iain Stirling's CelebAbility
programme.desc: Iain Stirling hosts this game show in which five friends take on five famous faces - beginning with Vicky Pattison, Pete Wicks, Olivia Buckland, Mikey North and Rickie Haywood-Williams - in a series of challenges based on unusual abilities that the celebrities claim they possess

programme.title: Hey Tracey!
programme.desc: Joel Dommett hosts the cold-calling comedy quiz in which his virtual PA connects celebrities to real people around the UK in the search for answers. Megan McKenna, Roman Kemp, Mark-Francis Vandelli, Melvin Odoom, Verona Rose and Christine McGuinness try to win some normal people £2,500 and a pet-food dispenser.

programme.title: The Masked Singer
programme.desc: Six unknown celebrities battle it out in the singing contest. The mystery contestants are dressed head to toe in elaborate costumes designed to hide their identities, while the panel - Jonathan Ross, Davina McCall, Rita Ora and Mo Gilligan - tries to guess who they are. In tonight's episode, a fresh batch of Masked Singers battle it out - Harlequin v Blob, Seahorse v Viking and Bush Baby v Grandfather Clock. Joel Dommett hosts the proceedings

programme.title: The Masked Singer: Unmasked
programme.desc: This episode includes all the extra gossip from the panel, an exclusive chat with our second unmasked celebrity and a behind-the-scenes look at what it takes to bring all the characters to life.

programme.title: Antz (1998)
programme.desc: A neurotic worker ant passes himself off as a soldier to impress a princess. Animated comedy, with the voices of Woody Allen, Sharon Stone, Gene Hackman and Sylvester Stallone

programme.desc: An unusual message appears in Chipping Cleghorn's local newspaper, revealing details of a forthcoming murder and, while at first glance it looks like a practical joke, the information proves to be deadly accurate. Feature-length drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Zoe Wanamaker, Elaine Paige, Virginia McKenna, Cherie Lunghi and Frances Barber

programme.desc: Barnaby and Troy set out to find a motive for a terminally ill property developer's murder. Many of the Badger's Drift residents have reason to hold a grudge against their charmless neighbour, including a theatre director with unhappy childhood memories, whom Cully gets the chance to work with. Mystery, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Richard Briers and Judy Parfitt

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective begins to suspect something is amiss when he visits one of his favoured haunts in London for dinner and discovers a fellow regular has dramatically changed his diet. Down in Brighton, elderly Anthony Gascoigne lies dying while his estranged twin brother Henry falls to his death - but nobody is sure whether the man was deliberately pushed. Bizarrely, the key to the riddle seems to lie in a blackberry pie. David Suchet stars in the title role, with Philip Jackson, Hugh Fraser and Pauline Moran

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The discovery of a body at a ramshackle farm alerts Wycliffe to the fact that a murderer is at large in a sleepy Cornish village - and when two locals disappear, he realises it will take all his deductive powers to solve the mystery. Whodunit, starring Jack Shepherd and Helen Masters

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A woman is murdered in a hedgerow but her son survives the attack, only to fall beneath the ice of a frozen pond as he makes his escape. DCI Vera Stanhope wades in and drags him out, and against all the odds he pulls through. As the investigation into the mother's killing unfolds, Vera finds a kindred spirit in the boy and comes to realise the key to solving the case lies deep in his memory. Detective drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, with Kerry Fox and Kieran O'Brien

programme.title: The Bay
programme.desc: With Nick seriously injured in hospital, Lisa furiously seeks out Sean, while Med feels increasingly side-lined. Sean's revelations lead the team to suspect Nick might have vital information about Holly and Dylan and they desperately wait for him to regain consciousness. Meanwhile, Rob attempts to leave the online forum, but finds that it is not as easy as he had hoped, and Sam tries to offer Abbie a way out from Vincent. Starring Morven Christie, Jonas Armstrong and Matthew McNulty

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective begins to suspect something is amiss when he visits one of his favoured haunts in London for dinner and discovers a fellow regular has dramatically changed his diet. Bizarrely, the key to the riddle seems to lie in a blackberry pie. David Suchet stars in the title role, with Philip Jackson, Hugh Fraser and Pauline Moran

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: When dock worker Owen Thorne plunges from the roof of a multi-storey car park while picking up his daughter from her school prom, Vera immediately detects foul play. The team soon discovers a work dispute over the death of a young man, with everyone believing Owen was to blame, and the victim's father admits he wanted revenge - but his alibi holds. The mystery then continues when Vera spots an inconsistency in the statement Owen's wife gave to the police, suggesting she's not certain of her husband's fidelity

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: The staff compete to win the coveted title of employee of the month, which carries a top prize of $500, as well as deck privileges, tours and extra time off. Deck hand Rhymond is among those with their eye on the top prize, having braved the elements since 5am to keep the ship looking pristine. Meanwhile, Timothy feels sad about leaving his friends on the front desk to start his new job with the shore excursions team, and engineer Scott is left in charge when his boss goes on leave, with jobs including making drinking water from the sea

programme.desc: Spurred into action by the discovery of a woman's body in Midsomer Worthy's Raven's Wood, Barnaby and Troy re-examine three similar unsolved murders in the same village nine years previously, hoping to get a lead before the killer strikes again. As they dig deeper into the clandestine world of the community, they find themselves embroiled in a sinister web of deceit, violence and mystery. programme.desc: A ritual killing in a village church points to the work of a satanic cult, but the detective thinks local animosity might actually hold the key to the mystery. Detective drama, starring Jack Shepherd

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective's former colleague, Sergeant Stuart Macken, is left badly burned when his house is petrol-bombed, while his teenage daughter fights for her life in intensive care. As Vera tries to discover why anyone would attempt to kill Stuart, her investigation leads to unpleasant revelations about his personal life, and she has to face long-buried memories of her own. Crime drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, with Steven Hartley and Julie Graham

programme.title: The Bay
programme.desc: A confession to Lisa shines a possible new light on Dylan's killer. Meanwhile, Abbie has a terrifying experience with some local addicts, and Rob's escalating actions begin to impact on his family. Elsewhere, Med starts to suspect Lisa is hiding something from him and begins to make his own investigations. Six-part crime drama following the work of Detective Sergeant Lisa Armstrong as she is assigned to a missing persons investigation. Starring Morven Christie, Jonas Armstrong, Tracie Bennett and Lindsey Coulson

programme.title: The Bay
programme.desc: Manning and Med question Holly about the mysterious other person present when Dylan died, determined to get to the truth. Lisa struggles to accept her suspension from the force after the reveal of her secret, but with everything at stake, will she still be able to bring Dylan's killer to justice from the sidelines? Final installment of the crime drama, starring Morven Christie and Jonas Armstrong

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A murder at a remote cottage in Northumberland takes DCI Vera Stanhope back to a place full of childhood memories and reminds her of an unsolved case from earlier in her career. Delving into the local politics surrounding a controversial proposal to dig a quarry, she and her team find motives in the unlikeliest of places - until they realise that the truth could be hidden within the land itself. Detective drama, adapted from Ann Cleeves' novel The Crow Trap, guest starring Daniela Nardini (This Life) alongside Brenda Blethyn

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: Captain Perrin throws a huge party to celebrate the ship's first birthday, with executive chef Shaun creating a giant cake that is so heavy it takes 10 men to carry - and they soon come unstuck when they cannot fit the ship-shaped sponge into the lift. Passengers Norman and Maria are looking forward to attending the lavish formal party, although they will be going nowhere unless front-desk supervisor Herbert can locate their missing luggage, which contains his tuxedo and her party frock. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth is called to Gossington Hall by her old friend Dolly Bantry (Joanna Lumley) when she finds the corpse of an unknown blonde in the library of her home. It soon transpires that there is no shortage of suspects, including local bad boy Basil Blake (Ben Miller) and Dolly's husband Arthur (James Fox), but when the victim is identified as a dancer in line to inherit a fortune, enquiries take a different turn. Crime drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Simon Callow, Jack Davenport and Tara Fitzgerald

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A walk in the woods leads to tragedy for Emily Simpson, who witnesses something unsettling before being found dead in her cottage by old friend Lucy Bellringer. Convinced natural causes are not to blame, Lucy calls in DCI Barnaby and Sergeant Troy, whose investigation eventually uncovers sinister long-buried secrets. The first ever episode of the long-running detective drama, guest starring Jessica Hynes and Emily Mortimer

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Community spirit in the village of Morton Fendle is dealt a blow by the collapse of a craft centre in which many locals had invested - and Alan Hollingsworth, the businessman behind the project, has become a public hate figure. Barnaby investigates when Hollingsworth's wife disappears, and seems to have been kidnapped. However, when Hollingsworth is subsequently murdered and his wife reappears safe and sound, he suspects an elaborate scheme. Roger Allam guest stars

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby visits a new age commune, where a man has fallen to his death, or may have been pushed. The dead man turns out to have a connection to the leader's shady past - they set the commune up together as a confidence trick, but the leader came to believe in what he preaches. A further death raises greater suspicion, especially since a vast inheritance might be coming the group's way. Guest starring Stephen Moyer (True Blood)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A woman's body is discovered in Captain Hastings' garage on Bonfire Night. It transpires that the deceased was due to marry an MP - and although the evidence suggests suicide, the Belgian detective suspects foul play. David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A series of scandals makes for an interesting few days in the quiet village of St Mary Mead, and most seem to revolve around a blustering local colonel, whose brusque manner and short temper make him unpopular with his fellow villagers. When he is found dead with a bullet through the head, Miss Marple and the reluctant DI Slack investigate. Whodunit, starring Geraldine McEwan as Miss Marple, with Jane Asher, Jason Flemying, Derek Jacobi and Miriam Margolyes

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the murder of social worker Jenny Lister, but figuring out the motive behind the killing of an apparently popular woman proves difficult. However, an infamous past case involving the death of a child appears to hold the key. In her pursuit of the truth, Vera uncovers the failings of an understaffed care system and discovers the secret at the heart of Jenny's death - which the killer will stop at nothing to protect. Drama based on the book by Ann Cleeves, starring Brenda Blethyn and David Leon

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A woman is murdered in a hedgerow but her son survives the attack, only to fall beneath the ice of a frozen pond as he makes his escape. DCI Vera Stanhope wades in and drags him out, and against all the odds he pulls through. As the investigation into the mother's killing unfolds, Vera finds a kindred spirit in the boy and comes to realise the key to solving the case lies deep in his memory. Detective drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, with Kerry Fox and Kieran O'Brien

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The mysterious shooting of archaeology student Celia Dawe and the significance of matching playing cards lead Wycliffe on a manhunt across Cornwall. Police drama based on the novel by WJ Burley, guest starring Bill Nighy alongside Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: Romance is in the air for casino host Cecilia and assistant maitre d' Alberto - and she already has clear ideas about the size of her engagement ring. Things are not so happy for waitress Valeria, whose relationship ended when her boyfriend left the ship and now a close friend is leaving too. However, it isn't long until a new beau catches her eye. Timothy came on board after splitting from his partner of four years - but he has little time to worry about his broken heart in his new job with the shore excursions team, especially when he runs into hot water with a couple of misplaced passengers

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin and Sally call in the police over Rosie's disappearance. Janice and Les Battersby move into number five with their family. Toyah and Leanne Battersby are caught shoplifting

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The Battersbys settle in to their new home and annoy the neighbours with their loud music. Bill and Rita are upset to learn that Sally has left Kevin

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim nags Steve to get a job. Donna resents the attention that Kelly is getting from Vic. Terry refuses to let Will join the Woolpack rugby team

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Betty suspects Mandy of stealing her washing. The Dingles take on the Woolpack pub quiz. Kelly announces that either Viv leaves or she does

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Hard-up local employer Albert Ross is suspected of stealing money from a blind man - and as events unfold, it begins to seem increasingly likely he is also putting profits before the welfare of his disgruntled workforce. Meanwhile, David risks his life to save dispirited musician Keith Drake from a watery grave, and inspires him to set about rebuilding his career

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid forgets an important party - unfortunately the occasion is his silver wedding anniversary. Sid James stars

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George pushes Mildred to choose between him and the dog when he begins to resent the affection lavished upon her beloved pooch

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: A faulty TV aerial prompts George to borrow his neighbour's ladder - but relations become strained when the henpecked husband falls off the roof and starts pointing the finger of blame. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: An unusual message appears in Chipping Cleghorn's local newspaper, revealing details of a forthcoming murder and, while at first glance it looks like a practical joke, the information proves to be deadly accurate. Miss Marple helps Inspector Craddock with his investigation and the pair soon uncover several likely suspects in the village. Feature-length drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Zoe Wanamaker, Elaine Paige, Virginia McKenna, Cherie Lunghi and Frances Barber

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Carol calls the police when she discovers Eva and James Knight's cottage has been vandalised, but the couple are reluctant to involve the law as Eva believes they are being targeted because she is Jewish. Meanwhile, an ancient traction steam engine called Goliath causes chaos in Aidensfield when it breaks down, and further mayhem arises when David takes it for a test run. Deborah Findlay and Tim Woodward guest

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Vic spends the night worrying about his relationship with Kelly. The Woolpack rugby team play their first match. Lisa finds out that Zak cheated in the quiz

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Dingles clear up after the collapse of their ceiling. Tara is horrified at the state of the Dingles' house. Steve finds his new labouring job tough

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maxine is impressed when Ashley tells her Fred is leaving him the business. Kevin is devastated when Bill tells him Sally won't have him back

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maxine feels guilty over kissing Steve. Leanne goes looking for a job at the Salon. Kevin is thrilled when Sally phones to say she's coming home

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Troy set out to find a motive for a terminally ill property developer's murder. Many of the Badger's Drift residents have reason to hold a grudge against their charmless neighbour, including a theatre director with unhappy childhood memories, whom Cully gets the chance to work with. Mystery, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Richard Briers and Judy Parfitt

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective begins to suspect something is amiss when he visits one of his favoured haunts in London for dinner and discovers a fellow regular has dramatically changed his diet. Down in Brighton, elderly Anthony Gascoigne lies dying while his estranged twin brother Henry falls to his death - but nobody is sure whether the man was deliberately pushed. Bizarrely, the key to the riddle seems to lie in a blackberry pie. David Suchet stars in the title role, with Philip Jackson, Hugh Fraser and Pauline Moran

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The discovery of a body at a ramshackle farm alerts Wycliffe to the fact that a murderer is at large in a sleepy Cornish village - and when two locals disappear, he realises it will take all his deductive powers to solve the mystery. Whodunit, starring Jack Shepherd and Helen Masters

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A woman is murdered in a hedgerow but her son survives the attack, only to fall beneath the ice of a frozen pond as he makes his escape. DCI Vera Stanhope wades in and drags him out, and against all the odds he pulls through. As the investigation into the mother's killing unfolds, Vera finds a kindred spirit in the boy and comes to realise the key to solving the case lies deep in his memory. Detective drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, with Kerry Fox and Kieran O'Brien

programme.title: The Bay
programme.desc: With Nick seriously injured in hospital, Lisa furiously seeks out Sean, while Med feels increasingly side-lined. Sean's revelations lead the team to suspect Nick might have vital information about Holly and Dylan and they desperately wait for him to regain consciousness. Meanwhile, Rob attempts to leave the online forum, but finds that it is not as easy as he had hoped, and Sam tries to offer Abbie a way out from Vincent. Starring Morven Christie, Jonas Armstrong and Matthew McNulty

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective begins to suspect something is amiss when he visits one of his favoured haunts in London for dinner and discovers a fellow regular has dramatically changed his diet. Down in Brighton, elderly Anthony Gascoigne lies dying while his estranged twin brother Henry falls to his death - but nobody is sure whether the man was deliberately pushed. Bizarrely, the key to the riddle seems to lie in a blackberry pie. David Suchet stars in the title role, with Philip Jackson, Hugh Fraser and Pauline Moran

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: When dock worker Owen Thorne plunges from the roof of a multi-storey car park while picking up his daughter from her school prom, Vera immediately detects foul play. The team soon discovers a work dispute over the death of a young man, with everyone believing Owen was to blame, and the victim's father admits he wanted revenge - but his alibi holds. The mystery then continues when Vera spots an inconsistency in the statement Owen's wife gave to the police, suggesting she's not certain of her husband's fidelity

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: The staff compete to win the coveted title of employee of the month, which carries a top prize of $500, as well as deck privileges, tours and extra time off. Deck hand Rhymond is among those with their eye on the top prize, having braved the elements since 5am to keep the ship looking pristine. Meanwhile, Timothy feels sad about leaving his friends on the front desk to start his new job with the shore excursions team, and engineer Scott is left in charge when his boss goes on leave, with jobs including making drinking water from the sea

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid's tries to join the pop scene make him a laughing stock and he realises the generation gap is wider than ever

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: The Ropers' television breaks down, and fixing it is a question of whether the British Museum has the parts in stock. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Poor George feels outnumbered when he learns Mildred's mother could be moving in. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan as the genteel sleuth. A woman experiences an unsettling vision after moving into the home of her dreams - and it transpires she lived in the house as a child. Miss Marple investigates the history of the property and discovers it was connected with a baffling disappearance years before - which has never been solved. With Dawn French, Russ Abbot, Geraldine Chaplin, Phil Davis and Sarah Parish

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Anti-fur protester Sue Padgett finds herself in deep trouble when her boyfriend and fellow crusader Nick Payton starts a fire at a haulier's depot. Carol and Chris fall out when they offer opposing advice to a pregnant teenager, while Peggy enthusiastically prepares David for a date with a farmer's ample daughter Tina Bestwick. David Roper and Amie Titterton guest star

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris isn't happy about the Dingles being at Home Farm. Zoe tells Kelly she is welcome to stay with her. Zak gets a shock when he opens the boot of Lisa's classic car

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Terry encourages Will to think more seriously about playing rugby. Butch and Zak argue about who is going to bury the body. Donna and Kirsty decide to get their noses pierced

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin sleeps on Bill's floor. Sally is cold towards Rita for not warning her about Kevin and Natalie. Roy demands his money back from Les

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin is stunned when Natalie tells him Sally slapped her. Percy starts to keep a record of the Battersbys' anti-social behaviour. Natalie pours out her heart to Audrey

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Spurred into action by the discovery of a woman's body in Midsomer Worthy's Raven's Wood, Barnaby and Troy re-examine three similar unsolved murders in the same village nine years previously, hoping to get a lead before the killer strikes again. As they dig deeper into the clandestine world of the community, they find themselves embroiled in a sinister web of deceit, violence and mystery. Guest starring Phyllis Logan, and featuring Toby Jones' first appearance in a recurring role as pathologist Dan Peterson

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: After enjoying a trip to the theatre with Captain Hastings, the detective is disturbed by the sound of gunshots in an apartment at Whitehaven Mansions, and soon finds himself investigating a murder. Drama, starring David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A ritual killing in a village church points to the work of a satanic cult, but the detective thinks local animosity might actually hold the key to the mystery. Detective drama, starring Jack Shepherd

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective's former colleague, Sergeant Stuart Macken, is left badly burned when his house is petrol-bombed, while his teenage daughter fights for her life in intensive care. As Vera tries to discover why anyone would attempt to kill Stuart, her investigation leads to unpleasant revelations about his personal life, and she has to face long-buried memories of her own. Crime drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, with Steven Hartley and Julie Graham

programme.title: The Bay
programme.desc: A confession to Lisa shines a possible new light on Dylan's killer. Meanwhile, Abbie has a terrifying experience with some local addicts, and Rob's escalating actions begin to impact on his family. Elsewhere, Med starts to suspect Lisa is hiding something from him and begins to make his own investigations. Six-part crime drama following the work of Detective Sergeant Lisa Armstrong as she is assigned to a missing persons investigation. Starring Morven Christie, Jonas Armstrong, Tracie Bennett and Lindsey Coulson

programme.title: The Bay
programme.desc: Manning and Med question Holly about the mysterious other person present when Dylan died, determined to get to the truth. Lisa struggles to accept her suspension from the force after the reveal of her secret, but with everything at stake, will she still be able to bring Dylan's killer to justice from the sidelines? Final installment of the crime drama, starring Morven Christie and Jonas Armstrong

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A murder at a remote cottage in Northumberland takes DCI Vera Stanhope back to a place full of childhood memories and reminds her of an unsolved case from earlier in her career. Delving into the local politics surrounding a controversial proposal to dig a quarry, she and her team find motives in the unlikeliest of places - until they realise that the truth could be hidden within the land itself. Detective drama, adapted from Ann Cleeves' novel The Crow Trap, guest starring Daniela Nardini (This Life) alongside Brenda Blethyn

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: Captain Perrin throws a huge party to celebrate the ship's first birthday, with executive chef Shaun creating a giant cake that is so heavy it takes 10 men to carry - and they soon come unstuck when they cannot fit the ship-shaped sponge into the lift. Passengers Norman and Maria are looking forward to attending the lavish formal party, although they will be going nowhere unless front-desk supervisor Herbert can locate their missing luggage, which contains his tuxedo and her party frock. Dancer Dulcie faces her staff appraisal, and a visit from her boyfriend helps with her homesickness

programme.desc: A major South American drugs gang is set to break into the UK trade, but Dempsey's concentration is weakened by the reappearance of an ex-girlfriend from the States. Detective drama, starring Michael Brandon
