programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to La Grange, a suburb of Chicago, to help the owners of Cafe 36, an upmarket bistro threatening to destroy the marriage of its owners due to the scale of their financial losses. The head chef's attitude to quality control is driving customers away, and it is up to Gordon Ramsay to turn the business around

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Folk dancer Gay and drama teacher Stephen are relocating to Finistère in the north west of France, where they love the rugged coastline and beautiful medieval villages. Laura Hamilton lines up five properties for their £100,000 budget, hoping at least one will be just what they are looking for

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Ian Snowley and Kay Marriot fell in love with the volcanic landscapes and coasts of Lanzarote, and are now determined to buy a house that they can use to explore the island further. Scarlette Douglas shows the couple five properties, hoping to maximise their budget of £150,000

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Guest-house owners in Spain hope their music event will not attract the local police. In France, a motorhome rental business deals with damaged vehicles and the owners of a holiday village in Portugal deal with an unfortunate leak

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer meet Nitasha and Nikunj in Staines-upon-Thames, who are unsure whether to sell their four-bed dormer bungalow or renovate instead. Having been reluctant to buy it just two and a half years ago, Nitasha has made the place home and believes it has the potential to be perfect for their future family. However, Nikunj feels like the work on the house will never be done, and would prefer to look for somewhere new

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: Make Do and Mend
programme.desc: Dick and Angel Strawbridge use the handiwork and design skills with which they have renovated their French chateau to give viewers ideas on fixing up their own homes. In the first show, Angel helps Miriam transform her 1970s bathroom into a relaxing sanctuary - without changing either the suite or the tiles - and Dick digs out his old handmade smoker to help teach Phil the ancient art of cheese smoking. The couple also advise Tom and Sim how to bring their unique front door into the 21st century

programme.title: The Man Who Fell from the Sky
programme.desc: In 2015, two men stowed away in the landing gear of a British Airways flight from Johannesburg to Heathrow. One of them fell to his death in Richmond - making worldwide headlines - but miraculously his companion survived an 11-hour journey with temperatures reaching -60 C. In this documentary, Richard Bentley sets out to uncover who they were, how they got on the plane, and why they decided to undertake such a dangerous and desperate mission

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows more of Britain's most ambitious self-building projects, as intrepid individuals attempt to design and construct the home of their dreams. In this first edition, Kevin meets former army captain Justin, who has spent £1.8million on an abandoned neo-Gothic cemetery keeper's lodge and adjoining ex-council toilet block in south-west London. Justin's vision is to restore the lodge but demolish the ugly toilet blocks and replace them with an enormous luxury ground-floor extension, with an added moat and a giant basement with a swimming pool - all just metres from the nearest grave

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: Architect George Clarke delves into the world of ingenious small builds, meeting people creating places to live, work and play in the most unlikely of places. Here he meets a couple who turned an old fire engine into a bar to raise money for firefighters' charities and a 23-year-old who built a shepherd's hut from scratch, including the nuts and bolts. George also visits an extraordinary home in Chile and Will Hardie builds a telescope

programme.title: How to Lose Weight Well
programme.desc: Doctors Helen Lawal and Javid Abdelmoneim help people try out the most popular diets on the market, with participants being put on a variety of regimens. Sisters Lesley and Debra from Essex road-test diets followed by celebrities Adele and Rebel Wilson, while friends Temi and Bose try out intermittent fasting, and drinking pals Maurice and Mick want to lose weight before attempting a Three Peaks walk in their native Yorkshire

programme.title: Britain's Most Expensive Houses
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes with UK Sotheby's International Realty, with the coronavirus pandemic having seen a surge of super-rich buyers leaving towns and cities. The brokers set out to sell a property in Highgate, north London, that is on the market for £40 million, as well the home of former Liverpool FC CEO Peter Moore on the Wirral

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Noel Fielding, Matt Lucas, Prue Leith and Paul Hollywood get the New Year celebrations started in the tent. Joining them are Helena and Henry from 2019, Nancy from 2014, and 2018 winner Rahul, all hoping to impress the judges with their skills and creativity and take home the coveted Festive Star Baker title

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at Wharfe House in Wetherby, run by B&B newcomer Pauline Cox, who's joined by her best friend Claire Smith. Eyebrows are raised when Mike and Karen Hepker arrive from Bath Lodge Castle in a chauffeur-driven car. During a visit to a local vineyard, sparks fly between Mike Hepker and Tash Thorne, who runs The Old Lodge Hotel in Gosport with her partner Mike Pearce. Having only just opened, Pauline has a house full for the very first time. The narrow antique beds cause concerns and Jenny and Clive Aylett from Wortwell Hall Barn in Norfolk are first to criticise breakfast. When it comes to feedback, Pauline's left deflated by her sausage scores - and Karen and Mike Hepker vote with their feet

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Part-time social worker Sara hosts the first dinner party from in and around Cumbria, hoping to impress with a menu of Middle Eastern cuisine in homage to her birthplace of Baghdad

programme.desc: Bricklayer Dale prepares an evening of construction-themed comfort food, including a Great Wall of Chinese Chicken, and Ash's flippant remarks get him in trouble

programme.desc: Horse-riding hospitality entrepreneur Ash hosts the penultimate party in Cumbria and chooses a menu of personal favourites, including king prawn pil pil, hoping to scoop the £1,000

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug turns the basement into a recreation room when his big-screen TV is delivered - until Carrie's father and sister move in. Pilot episode of the comedy, starring Kevin James and Leah Remini

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: After stumbling on a photo of Carrie's overweight mother, Doug pretends to go on a diet to persuade his wife to lose a few pounds. Kevin James stars

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Fiery chef Gordon Ramsay comes to the aid of a mother-and-son team who have gone through 20 chefs in two years at their struggling Italian restaurant in California. The adjoining bar is a triumph, but the oldest dining establishment in town needs an overhaul if the inexperienced owners are to make a profit

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lorna and Gordon are relocating and looking for their new dream home around the seaside town of Benalmádena on Spain's Costa del Sol. For their budget of £250,000, they want enough space for their family of 19 children and grandchildren to come and visit. Ben Hillman shows them five great options, but will any measure up to the couple's idea of perfection?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Su and Reg Roberts from south Wales first fell in love with Tobago 20 years ago and have been back every other year since. Now, with their children growing up and retirement approaching, they're ready to turn their dream of a holiday home on the island into a reality. Presenter Scarlette Douglas shows them five properties within their £310,000 budget

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: As the season draws to a close, the Brits abroad reflect on a strange summer. In Spain, a guest house tries to tempt Spanish visitors with local cuisine and a cycling retreat wins some much-needed business, while a Portuguese holiday village takes delivery of a massive new centrepiece

programme.title: Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels across the United States in search of restaurants in dire need of renovation - and after analysing their situation, he has just 24 hours to bring them back from the brink of disaster. In this opening edition, Gordon must revamp Lowery's Seafood in Tappahannock, Virginia, where his crew find two brothers - one the owner and the other the manager - at odds with each other

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.desc: First of a two-part special taking viewers behind the scenes of a Cambridgeshire police investigation into a woman who is plotting to have her ex-husband killed. A man handed police a secretly recorded conversation that appeared to show his girlfriend offering to pay someone to commit murder, and DS Mike Barnshaw has to work out whether the recording is evidence that she really wanted him dead or is pillow talk with a man she has picked up on a dating site

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.desc: In the conclusion of this two-part documentary, Victoria Breeden is taken into custody, but claims she is not fit to be interviewed, while police search her home for evidence that proves she has attempted to hire someone to murder her husband. Detectives are determined not to have to release her, but DC Louisa Abbott fears she is dealing with the cleverest criminal she has ever encountered

programme.title: The Big Fat Quiz of Everything
programme.desc: Rob Beckett, Roisin Conaty, Nish Kumar, Katherine Ryan, Oti Mabuse and Rob Brydon join Jimmy Carr as he poses questions on anything and everything, with subjects including Only Fools and Horses, Johnny Cash and US tourist attractions. As always, Jimmy is helped by newsreader Jon Snow, actor Charles Dance and the children from Mitchell Brook Primary School

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics cast their eyes over 2020's biggest Christmas TV shows, reviewing the most talked about hits from the festive season, including classic movies, exciting new dramas and some of telly's biggest traditional treats

programme.title: Jamie and Jimmy's Friday Night Feast
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver and Jimmy Doherty present dishes for the weekend from their cafe on Southend Pier. Guest Jack Dee revisits his days as a chef at the Ritz and shares memories of family camping holidays in France as a child. The hosts track down the campsite Jack's family stayed at and recreate the effect of a perfect rotisserie chicken, but in a domestic oven. Jamie also serves up a pot-roast pork wrapped in cured meat with sage and garlic and a side of celeriac, spinach and compote apple and rhubarb, while Jimmy rustles up an easy way of doing spicy veggie samosas

programme.title: Swingers
programme.desc: Documentary about the scene in which people engage in group sex or swap sexual partners, going behind the scenes at one of Britain's most popular swingers' clubs to find out what kinds of people attend and why

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: This competition comes from in and around Newcastle, where first up is 25-year-old barista Delphinium, who's eager to prove that vegetarians can win with her travel-inspired menu. The guests she's hoping to impress this week are 64-year-old financial advisor David, art teacher Karen, development manager Mark and 34-year-old mobile hairdresser Deborah. It's an unusual evening complete with strange food, shocking name reveals and dessert taking an unexpected turn

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the second night of the competition in and around Newcastle, and art teacher Karen's turn to host. She's looking to impress her guests with a ginger-themed menu. Fussy eater Debs is unexcited at the prospect of goat's cheese or salmon en croute and is more concerned whether Karen has made her own pastry, and while vegetarian Delphinium is underwhelmed by Karen's menu, she really gets stuck into Karen's art class entertainment. Software salesman Mark winds Debs up about 64-year-old ladies' man David, who likes to shock the group with his antics in Ibiza

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Newcastle, mobile hairdresser and amateur musical songstress Debbie plans to bring showbiz razzle dazzle to her night. Humble pie from Mark shocks Karen, and there are very unusual dumplings for barista Delph and Ibiza-loving David to try

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the fourth day in and around Newcastle, Ibiza-loving 64-year-old David plans an elaborate Ibizan-style menu of tapas and paella. However, the menu is all a bit much for Mark, and there's still the issue of Karen's bruised leg and bruised ego to deal with

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the final night from in and around Newcastle, and the turn of Essex boy Mark to impress his new Geordie chums with a menu of deep fried brie, slow-cooked lamb and a passion fruit pavlova. Debs, again, struggles with the menu but also lets out her personal feelings about the group. Art teacher Karen gets it in the neck for ruining other people's nights, Mark is accused of being boring, and David settles for bringing a very weird noise to the table

programme.title: Coach Trip
programme.desc: Seven more couples board the coach, heading off on an epic 30-day road trip around the Mediterranean, with Brendan Sheerin acting as tour guide

programme.title: Coach Trip
programme.desc: The travellers arrive in Mallorca, and the group are buzzing after going flyboarding, but tour guide Brendan Sheerin brings them down a peg or two at an ostrich farm

programme.title: Coach Trip
programme.desc: The travellers embark on their third day travelling around the Mediterranean, as Brendan takes the group to a chorizo-making class before they have a go at rock climbing

programme.title: Coach Trip
programme.desc: The travellers go riding quad bikes and Brendan indulges them with a massage class, with mixed results. At the vote, one couple make their feelings about everyone else very clear

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman shows five properties to recently married Londoners Katy and Dom, who want to buy a holiday home in the Cadiz area of south-west Spain. With a budget of up to £180,000, will any of the five properties Jasmine has lined up to show them be their perfect place in the sun?

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: The return of the programme following British expats as they set up new businesses in France, Spain, Italy and Portugal. A London couple roll out plans for their new motorhome business in France, while in Spain, a couple work hard to fill their empty guest house

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: In Portugal, holiday village owners rush to get ready in time for their very first guests, while a motorhome rental business in France encounters some technical difficulties

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: A couple of bike lovers launch a new cycling business in Spain, while in France, a motorhome rental business deals with a busy day of bookings

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Guest-house owners in Spain hope their music event will not attract the local police. In France, a motorhome rental business deals with damaged vehicles and the owners of a holiday village in Portugal deal with an unfortunate leak

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: As the season draws to a close, the Brits abroad reflect on a strange summer. In Spain, a guest house tries to tempt Spanish visitors with local cuisine and a cycling retreat wins some much-needed business, while a Portuguese holiday village takes delivery of a massive new centrepiece

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits some of the restaurants that he set out to save, including The Mixing Bowl, The Olde Stone Mill, Peter's and Campania. Have their owners pulled themselves out of their nightmares?

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Scottsdale, Arizona, where he meets husband-and-wife bistro owners Samy and Amy Bouzaglo, who reveal a shocking contempt for customer feedback. The chef faces the biggest challenge of the series to save Amy's Baking Company, but can he get through to this defiant pair?

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics cast their eyes over 2020's biggest Christmas TV shows, reviewing the most talked about hits from the festive season, including classic movies, exciting new dramas and some of telly's biggest traditional treats

programme.title: Tom Allen Goes to Town
programme.desc: The comedian visits the West Yorkshire town of Wakefield, taking in one of the world's biggest rhubarb crops, exploring its beautiful cathedral and visiting one of the only drive-in fish and chip shops in the world. He also buys a round of Wakefield's famous steak pies and helps renovate the nearby Caribbean restaurant for the family he's been staying with. At the Theatre Royal, Tom is joined by a special assistant for a comedy show - none other than Spandau Ballet's Martin Kemp - entertaining host a full house of locals

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Noel Fielding, Matt Lucas, Prue Leith and Paul Hollywood get the New Year celebrations started in the tent. Joining them are Helena and Henry from 2019, Nancy from 2014, and 2018 winner Rahul, all hoping to impress the judges with their skills and creativity and take home the coveted Festive Star Baker title

programme.title: Jamie and Jimmy's Friday Night Feast
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver and Jimmy Doherty present dishes for the weekend from their cafe on Southend Pier. Guest Jack Dee revisits his days as a chef at the Ritz and shares memories of family camping holidays in France as a child. The hosts track down the campsite Jack's family stayed at and recreate the effect of a perfect rotisserie chicken, but in a domestic oven. Jamie also serves up a pot-roast pork wrapped in cured meat with sage and garlic and a side of celeriac, spinach and compote apple and rhubarb, while Jimmy rustles up an easy way of doing spicy veggie samosas

programme.title: How to Lose Weight Well
programme.desc: Doctors Helen Lawal and Javid Abdelmoneim help people try out the most popular diets on the market, with participants being put on a variety of regimens. Sisters Lesley and Debra from Essex road-test diets followed by celebrities Adele and Rebel Wilson, while friends Temi and Bose try out intermittent fasting, and drinking pals Maurice and Mick want to lose weight before attempting a Three Peaks walk in their native Yorkshire

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows more of Britain's most ambitious self-building projects, as intrepid individuals attempt to design and construct the home of their dreams. In this first edition, Kevin meets former army captain Justin, who has spent £1.8million on an abandoned neo-Gothic cemetery keeper's lodge and adjoining ex-council toilet block in south-west London. Justin's vision is to restore the lodge but demolish the ugly toilet blocks and replace them with an enormous luxury ground-floor extension, with an added moat and a giant basement with a swimming pool - all just metres from the nearest grave

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Wiltshire mother and daughter Emma and Charlotte have a budget of £100,000 for a holiday home in Antequera in Spain where they hope to spend more quality time together as a family. Jasmine Harman pulls out all the stops to show five suitable properties

programme.title: World's Strongest Man
programme.desc: James Richardson presents the final from Haitang Bay in Sanya, China, featuring the 10 qualifiers from the heats. Zydrunas Savickas of Lithuania started as the reigning champion, having won the title for the third time last year, but a strong contingent of Americans were also expected to challenge for glory, including 2011 victor Brian Shaw and the 2012 America's Strongest Man Mike Burke

programme.title: World's Strongest Man 2014
programme.desc: Action from the opening round of the Giants Live qualifying campaign, which came from Melbourne, Australia, as the quest to reach the World's Strongest Man contest commenced. Britain's Rob Frampton was part of a quality field that also included the likes of Australia's Warrick Brant and Icelandic contender Hafthor Julius Bjornsson

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: Another selection of residential feuds, featuring Barnsley pensioners Barry and Phyllis Roddis's dispute with a neighbour who was scrapping cars in his driveway, which led to him waging a nine-year campaign of intimidation against them. Plus, in Streatham, south London, Richard and Philomena Chadwick have been battling for five years to evict squatters whose bongo-playing parties and rooftop sunbathing have invaded the privacy of their once secluded garden

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: A family who have generated more than 600 complaints from angry neighbours move into a quiet suburban street in Blackburn. Plus, a psychiatrist in south London battles a huge renovation next door, while thumping basslines, pounding walls and slamming doors deprive a mother and her family of sleep

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Stewart and Elmor are in Manchester to recover £4,000 owed to a firm of architects, only for the debtor to attempt to throw them off the scent. Stewart is joined by Vic in Liverpool to track down a landlord who owes his former tenants deposits totalling £5,000, while Gareth and Mitch are in for a surprise when they chase a builder in Wiltshire

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Matt and Garry try to recover more than £6,000 owed in nursery fees in Warwickshire, while Gary and Cona head to West Sussex, chasing £11,000 owed by a businessman who has defaulted on a previous plan, and have no option but to break into the property. The pair also head down to the south coast of England, seeking more than £3,500 owed for an unpaid loan

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Matt and Garry are in Wolverhampton, where a woman who owes £2,000 in parking fines denies knowing the debtor - despite being a relative. But when her mother also gets involved, tempers start to fray. Stewart and Iain confront a woman in Cheshire who is refusing to pay fees to a veterinary practice, claiming her dog has been misdiagnosed, while Paul and Max collect £5,000 owed by a banned company director in east London accused of mis-selling assets on the stock market

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Gary and Paul visit Kent to recover £2,500 owed by a publican to a utilities supplier - only to find themselves in the middle of a family at war. Stewart and Elmor deal with a sensitive case in Liverpool, as they repossess a property where the tenant has failed to pay any rent for almost a year

programme.title: Benefits Britain: Life on the Dole
programme.desc: This edition of the programme focuses on large families who depend on the benefits system, including 64-year-old Peter Rolfe, whose children - by 15 different women - range in ages from three to 43, of which he is the sole carer for six. He has been fighting his council in the Isle of Wight for a much bigger house and is also at loggerheads with the benefits office for capping his weekly payment. West Midlands mother-of-eight Marie Buchanan has recently split from her partner and is looking for a larger home, while Ipswich couple the Fisks have been in dispute with their neighbours, who objected to two houses being knocked into one to accommodate them and their 13 children

programme.title: Extreme Nightmare Tenants
programme.desc: A letting agent uncovers a cannabis farm when inspecting one of his properties, and a landlady struggles to gain access to a property that was rented by an extreme hoarder who has recently died. Sci-fi thriller, starring Ewan McGregor, Scarlett Johansson, Sean Bean and Steve Buscemi

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: Documentary using eyewitness testimony and footage from camcorders, CCTV and phones to examine the experiences of people who have become involved in disputes with neighbours. Posters aimed at naming and shaming one of the residents of Withycombe, Somerset, for taking part in a government-backed badger cull result in him and his family being driven out by animal rights activists. In Woodford Green, north-east London, an ambitious house extension results in all light being blocked to one neighbouring property and huge cracks appearing in the other, while a Bournemouth woman ends up having a restraining order taken out against her after erecting a gated driveway before receiving official approval

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: Close friends Janet Perrie and Fiona Hope moved to the South Ayrshire village of Dipple in search of an idyllic new life for themselves and their 22 dogs. Unfortunately for them, other residents were enraged by the noise, smell and mess and wasted no time in contacting the authorities. In a cul-de-sac in Leicestershire, the Pollard, Walne and Elliott families have been harassed by 68-year-old Leslie Collins for more than a decade after they complained about the noise from an illegal wood-waste business he was running from his home. He then began to target them with increasingly intimidating behaviour - including mooning, two-finger salutes, shouting abuse, cutting holes in hedges and spying on them with binoculars

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Matt and Garry are in Wolverhampton, where a woman who owes £2,000 in parking fines denies knowing the debtor - despite being a relative. programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Documentary series giving an insight into the world of debt collection from the perspectives of debtors, creditors and those who collect the payments. Brian and Graham have been warned to expect trouble as they evict some tenants from a south London flat, one of whom has a reputation for violence. Everything goes smoothly until the enforcement agents make a shocking discovery in a child's bedroom. Paul and Steve head to Slough to collect wages owed to a freelance worker, but the debtor is less than happy to find the high-court enforcement officers on his doorstep and things get physical

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Sheriff Paul Bohill tackles the case of a small debt owed by ex-footballer Neil 'Razor' Ruddock to a kennel owner, before being forced to evict a school dinner lady in south London. Meanwhile, in the north of England enforcement agent Stewart MacCracken heads out with trainee Iain Taylor to confront a Yorkshire builder they need to get payment from at his home, while a reptile shop owner hit by online competitors urgently needs to pay a debt for the feed company he relies on to keep his animals alive

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Enforcement officers are faced with the difficult job of evicting a woman with two children - a task that takes a turn for the worse when her ex-partner makes an appearance. In Liverpool, locals threaten a riot over an unpaid debt at a computer shop, while two agents come across an extremely rare car in the course of their duties

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: The efforts of north London landlord Franc to evict a tenant withholding rent payments on a property whose boiler mysteriously stopped working whenever the rent was due, and a tenant with asthma who has been forced to sleep on his sofa for two years in a damp flat. Plus, a landlord owed £7,000 in rent discovers her nightmare is not over when she evicts the tenant and gains access to her property

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: A man living in Cumbria takes issue with his neighbour's habit of letting his menagerie of chickens, cockerels, parrots and other birds freely wander along a residential road, and is even more disturbed by the fact that some of his fellow residents don't seem to have a problem with it. A former urban couple who moved to a rural valley in north Wales to breed horses enter open conflict with the owners of a nearby yurt business, whose fireworks parties spooked one of their prized beasts, resulting in an injury. programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: The documentary focuses on two residential disputes that escalated from complaints about noise-making to far more serious quarrels. Warren and Sharee detail how their peaceful lives in a quiet Birmingham suburb were ruined when new neighbour Harry moved in. Harry complained of noise the pair knew wasn't coming from their household, an accusation he would level more than once, and with increasing intensity. Finally, when the hostile Harry pulled out a gun and threatened the couple over their garden fence, they were forced to sell up and move out. However, even after relocating, it seemed their nightmare was not over. Also featured is the story of Jill, who was just a teenager when Barry, who lived with his parents next-door, became fixated on her family making a racket. Brushing off the grievance, Jill and her relatives tried to get on with their lives, but one autumn day the feud took a tragic, life-changing turn

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: A group of residents in Guildford object when the occupant of a one-bedroom council flat decides to transform his home into a 'cannabis cafe'. In a small community in Wales, new arrivals cause a stir when they decide to convert a local pub into their new home, and in Lincolnshire, a young father becomes the victim of a violent assault after asking the man living in the flat below his to keep the noise down

Detective drama, with Peter Falk, Clive Revill, Jeanette Nolan and Bernard Behrens

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: A popular girl at an exclusive school is murdered and an examination of her clothes reveals evidence of a substance that Adam believes could only have come from Mars. The detectives discover a male pupil is in possession of a lizard that may be responsible for the strange traces and an exploration of his link to the victim uncovers incidences of jealousy and bullying

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: An old man is fatally shot on a subway platform, but the investigation reveals the victim is in fact a woman wearing an elaborate disguise. Inquiries fail to reveal her identity, but Hawkes makes an intriguing discovery regarding the nature of the murder weapon, providing the team with a more viable lead. Meanwhile, Jo decides the time is right to tell daughter Ellie the truth about her biological mother

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Hawkes is persuaded by his girlfriend Camille to call in sick so he can join her at a food festival, but a stall explodes during the event - exposing his deception to his colleagues. programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Curtis investigate the murder of a rare coin dealer, focusing their efforts on a millionaire who seems the most likely culprit - but Ross finds himself forced to play detective too. Sam Waterson and Jerry Orbach star

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: An investigation into a hit-and-run leads to a property scam, but Green, Rubirosa and Lupo's undercover work takes a surprising twist that pits McCoy against the US Attorney's office. January Jones (Mad Men) guest stars

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A riot at an immigration rally in Central Park ends in murder, and the political climate forces McCoy to assign a special prosecutor, who starts to increase tensions in the office with his anti-establishment attitude. Police drama, starring Sam Waterston and Jesse L Martin

programme.desc: Gibbs entrusts the team with the task of finding his former commanding officer, who has gone on the run rather than staying behind to face charges of killing a marine and stealing funds. programme.desc: As Gibbs recovers from his gunshot wound, the team is called in to investigate the killing of a US Marine and tries to determine whether the crime is related to an alleged case of blackmail at an illegal nightclub. Drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.desc: Benson and the team investigate the death of a prostitute at a pop-up brothel. Drama, starring Mariska Hargitay, Kelli Giddish and Ice-T

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.desc: Zak Bagans, host of Ghost Adventures, fulfils a lifelong dream of opening a museum in downtown Las Vegas, full of the haunted and cursed objects he has been collecting through the years. Here, Zak comes face to face with a 100-year-old doll who inspired a horror movie franchise. Then, he consults a Las Vegas rabbi after examining a deadly wine cabinet with a connection to the Holocaust

programme.title: Modern Times
programme.desc: Documentary about the characters who live and work in the area of central London, which is famous for being the most coveted square on the Monopoly board and is home to Claridge's hotel and Christie's auction house. The film reveals a world of surprises and deep contrasts, including an estate agent selling multimillion-pound mansions, cafe owners hanging on to their livelihoods by their fingernails, an NHS doctor whose patients range from billionaires to people on benefits, an upmarket resident who dresses in Victorian clothing and a cabbie living in social housing

Second World War adventure, starring Jack Hawkins and John Gregson

programme.desc: Part one of two. An alcoholic detective pursues the infamous serial killer brutally murdering prostitutes in Victorian London. programme.title: The Cube
programme.desc: Liverpudlian June is confident she can do well in the contest, having practised at home with her sister, while IT project manager Chris, from Edinburgh, aims to win enough money to put down a deposit on a house. Phillip Schofield provides support as they tackle a range of challenges, from agility tests to skill trials, under pressure to hold their nerve as friends, family and the studio audience watch their every move

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Contestants include Rickie Haywood-Williams, Melvin Odoom, Carol Smillie, Andrea Mclean, Katy Brand, Su Pollard, Syd Little and Eddie Large

programme.title: All Star Family Fortunes
programme.desc: X Factor 2009 winner Joe McElderry and actress Jo Joyner take part in the game show, teaming up with their families to guess the answers to surveys of 100 people in a bid to reach the final round, where they can win up to £30,000 for a charity of their choice. Vernon Kay hosts

programme.title: The Cube
programme.desc: The game show returns in a midweek slot, with a host of fresh games designed to test contestants' skill, nerve and determination. First up is Derry-Londonderry radiographer Lindsey, who believes that working in the trauma department of Southampton Hospital will help her stay calm under pressure. She is followed by retired Yorkshireman Keith, who at 70 is the oldest person to have appeared on the show. Will his extra years give him an advantage in defeating the Cube? Phillip Schofield hosts

programme.title: The Cube
programme.desc: The high-pressure contest continues as Phillip Schofield invites more members of the public to step into the Cube for another selection of agility tests and skill trials that could see them score a jackpot of £250,000. This time, Welsh farmer Rhodri Jones hopes to win the cash prize to buy a prized bull, and barista Zoe Webster has her sights set on taking her family on a dream holiday

programme.title: The Chase

programme.title: NFL: America's Game
programme.desc: The story of Baltimore Ravens' 2000 Super Bowl success over New York Giants at the Raymond James Stadium in Tampa, Florida. The Ravens became the second team in four years to win the Super Bowl after qualifying for the play-offs as one of the wildcard teams, and the 34-7 victory was the first for the franchise since leaving Cleveland under a huge cloud of controversy

programme.title: NFL: America's Game
programme.desc: Revisiting Dallas Cowboys' 1995 Super Bowl victory over Pittsburgh Steelers at the Sun Devil Stadium in Tempe, Arizona. Dallas tied the record set by the San Francisco 49ers in the previous year by claiming their fifth Vince Lombardi trophy with a 27-17 victory. They also became the first team to win the showpiece match three times in four years
